events.id: Jason Bonham’s Led Zeppelin Evening-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Jason Bonham’s Led Zeppelin Evening
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Jason Bonham’s Led Zeppelin Evening (JBLZE) is coming to The Opera House for a night of rock and roll classics. The landmark tour will let fans experience hits such as ‘Kashmir’ and ‘The Wanton Song’ live alongside deeper cuts like ‘In The Light’ and ‘Boogie With Stu.’ Along with playing the iconic album in its entirety, other Led Zeppelin favourites, including ‘Good Times Bad Times,’ ‘Whole Lotta Love”, and of course ‘Stairway To Heaven’, find their way into the setlist. 
“For me, this isn’t just an album — it’s the album,” declares Jason Bonham. “Physical Graffiti has always been my ultimate Led Zeppelin record, and I set out to honour its 50th Anniversary with 50 unforgettable shows." Jason is the son of Led Zeppelin drummer John Bonham. Since his father’s death, he has performed with the three surviving members of Led Zeppelin on several occasions.

events.id: Te Wānanga o Raukawa Pulse Netball Season 2026--Wellington NZ
events.name: Te Wānanga o Raukawa Pulse Netball Season 2026
events.long_description:
What’s tough, fast, and physical? Wellington’s netball team, Te Wānanga o Raukawa Pulse. After reaching the semi-finals last year, the Pulse are hungry to dominate the ANZ Premiership this year. Their campaign kicks off in April and continues through to June. Five of the ten games are played on home soil, either at the TSB Arena on Wellington’s waterfront, Te Rauparaha Arena in Porirua or Fly Palmy Arena in Palmerston North. 
Netball is known for its speed, agility, deft footwork, and pinpoint feeds. As a live spectator sport, it always delivers. This year, Khiarna Williams, Ameliaranne Ekenasio and Ine-Marí Venter provide the fireworks at the shooting end of the court. Head court-side to cheer them on and feel the energy pulse.

events.id: Circa Theatre Season 2026-Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Circa Theatre Season 2026
events.venue: Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Inside Circa Theatre’s waterfront venue, you’ll find two performance spaces. Both offer a smorgasbord of professional theatre throughout the year. Playwrights from international heavy hitters to brilliant Kiwi voices are represented. It also includes programming from comedies and cabarets to contemporary dance and pantomime. 
Before or after your show, visit the on-site café, brasserie and restaurant, Chouchou. It opens out onto a deck, where you can take in views across the harbour. Look up to admire the art installation above. Called ‘Velarium’, it undulates in the wind, mirroring the water in the harbour. 
Circa Theatre began its life in 1976. A breakaway group of actors and theatre practitioners decided that Aotearoa needed a new and innovative approach to theatre. Five decades later, it is still pushing boundaries, making people laugh and cry, and creating theatrical memories. 
If you don’t have your own Circa memory yet, there’s an opportunity to create one nearly every night of the week.

events.id: Breathe | Mauri Ora-Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Breathe | Mauri Ora
events.venue: Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description: Breathe | Mauri Ora is an immersive journey into nature. The exhibition comprises of major video works on a grand, awe-inspiring scale. With guided meditation from the likes of Cate Blanchett and evocative works on paper, Breathe | Mauri Ora invites visitors to slow down, relax, and breathe. Connect to the natural world like never before. Gain a deep understanding of nature’s interconnectedness and our place within it.

events.id: New Zealand Symphony Orchestra 2026 Season-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: New Zealand Symphony Orchestra 2026 Season
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The NZSO’s reputation for artistic excellence attracts many of the world’s leading musicians, conductors, and soloists. In 2026, some of the music-world luminaries include violinists María Dueñas and James Ehnes, trombonist Jörgen van Rijen, pianist Isata Kanneh-Mason, and New Zealand piano prodigy Shan Liu.   
Wellington shows are hosted in the Michael Fowler Centre, home of the NZSO. The touring programme will see the orchestra perform over 100 concerts in over 30 communities. Be among over 100,000 people across the motu who experience live orchestra music.

events.id: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Mills Albert Wellington Saints take to the court for what is sure to be a massive 2026 season. After taking out the 2025 season with a nail-biting win over the Southland Sharks, can they defend their championship title? Kiwi coach Aaron Young takes to the helm again after winning coach of the year. Hutt Valley local Jordan Hunt returns to the team after representing New Zealand in the Tall Blacks, and Melbourne’s Akech Aliir joins the Saints, bringing 6’9” of power and athleticism. 
The 2026 regular season features nine home games at the TSB Arena. Saints’ fans can expect high-energy basketball and a family-friendly atmosphere. Tip-off times have been optimised to make it easier for young fans to witness their heroes in action. 
Join the game court-side and put your lungs to the test as you cheer on your favourite Wellington side.

events.id: Book Launch: Finding Cynthia Winters by Sue Watson-57 Willis St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Book Launch: Finding Cynthia Winters by Sue Watson
events.venue: 57 Willis St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
About the event
Launching at Unity Books, Finding Cynthia Winters is a "powerful, moving, and darkly-honest memoir".
Sue Watson grew up in a perfect, loving nuclear family, a white-picket fence, quarter acre paradise. But all was not quite as it seemed. Sue and her sister were adopted, and one day when Sue as a young woman was studying in Wellington, she received a letter from her birth mother Elizabeth Winters. This began a life-long journey to discover the shadowy truth of her past.
As per, all welcome! See you at the signing table.
* * * * * * * * * * * * * * *
Finding Cynthia also premiers at BATS Theater on 16th April. Tickets are available through BATS - https://bats.co.nz/whats-on/finding-cynthia/

events.id: Jurassic Park - Eat the film-5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Jurassic Park - Eat the film
events.venue: 5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Designed to showcase the incredibly creative and ever-changing menu of our playful, fine dining establishment CoCo At The Roxy. EAT THE FILM provides film and food fans with a cult classic film and delicious seasonal and thematic dishes* designed to arrive in-theatre, perfectly timed to specific scenes.
All tickets include several themed dishes and drinks brought to you in the theatre, an experience with more than enough to sate your eyes and appetite.
This menu will be in keeping with the herbivore dinosaurs and will be fully vegetarian.
--- In Steven Spielberg's massive blockbuster, paleontologists Alan Grant (Sam Neill) and Ellie Sattler (Laura Dern) and mathematician Ian Malcolm (Jeff Goldblum) are among a select group chosen to tour an island theme park populated by dinosaurs created from prehistoric DNA. While the park's mastermind, billionaire John Hammond (Richard Attenborough), assures everyone that the facility is safe, they find out otherwise when various ferocious predators break free and go on the hunt. ---
Schedule:
6:00pm – The Roxy main doors open
6:00pm – Themed pre-show upstairs in the Grand Lobby
6:20pm – Cinema doors open
6:30pm – Quick intro
6:35pm – Feature begins
When purchased tickets either select ETF - Alcohol for the Cocktail option or ETF - Non-Alc for the Mocktail version of drinks.
*No dietaries catered for.
Presented in 4K

events.id: Learn to Lindy Hop: Beginner and intermediate-Corner of Colombo & Rintoul Streets, Newtown, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Learn to Lindy Hop: Beginner and intermediate
events.venue: Corner of Colombo & Rintoul Streets, Newtown, Wellington
events.long_description:
Learn to Lindy Hop with Sugarfoot Stomp!
Lindy Hop is a Black American social partnered dance born from the jazz and blues era of the 1930s-1940s. Join us each week to learn some of the foundational rhythms, shapes, and lead/follow concepts to get you swingin’ out on the dance floor!
Class blocks are on a 6 week rotation and designed to get you moving and grooving no matter your skill or prior experience. You can jump in at any time - no need to wait for the start of a block.
No partner required. Drop-ins are welcome!
Tuesdays:
6:30 pm | Level Up Your Lindy: Intermediate (doors open 6:20pm)
7:45 pm | Learn To Lindy Hop: Beginner
Venue:
Te Whare Hapori o ngā Puna Waiora | Newtown Community Centre.
Price:
Dop-in | $20
6 week block pass | $75 non-members, $60 members
First class free! If this is your first time ever dancing with Sugarfoot Stomp and you're not sure you want to commit to a whole block, your first class is on us!
Please ensure you meet the requirements on our website to attend the intermediate class. See "Which Class Should I Attend?"
Love to see you there!
You can visit our website to register ahead of time: https://www.sugarfootstomp.co.nz/classes

events.id: Ride the Cyclone-1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Ride the Cyclone
events.venue: 1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Darkly funny, wildly imaginative, and surprisingly heartfelt, Ride the Cyclone is the viral musical phenomenon that has taken the world by storm.
In this hilarious and exhilarating story, the lives of six teenagers of the St. Cassian Chamber Choir are changed forever after they board a doomed rollercoaster ride.
Thrown into the abyss, they awake to find an ominous mechanical fortune teller that offers them the chance to compete for the prize … to return home in one piece.
Now confronted with their darkest desires, shameful secrets and each other, this “supremely witty … and just plain delightful” (New York Times) musical makes a uniquely uplifting and outrageous case for what makes a life worth living.

events.id: Jason Bonham's Led Zeppelin Evening New Zealand Tour 2026 | Opera House, Wellington-The Opera House, 109 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Jason Bonham's Led Zeppelin Evening New Zealand Tour 2026 | Opera House, Wellington
events.venue: The Opera House, 109 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Jason Bonham’s Led Zeppelin Evening Announces New Zealand as part of the World Tour Celebrating the 50th Anniversary of Physical Graffiti this April 2026
Jason Bonham’s Led Zeppelin Evening/JBLZE is announcing the final dates to Celebrate the 50th Anniversary of the Led Zeppelin classic, Physical Graffiti and what better way to wrap up the World Tour than with dates down under! The landmark tour will let fans experience classics such as “Kashmir” and “The Wanton Song” live alongside deeper cuts like “In The Light” and “Boogie With Stu.” Along with playing the iconic album in its entirety, other Led Zeppelin favourites including “Good Times Bad Times,” “Whole Lotta Love” and of course “Stairway To Heaven” find their way into the setlist.
“For me, this isn’t just an album—it’s the album,” declares Jason Bonham. “Physical Graffiti has always been my ultimate Led Zeppelin record, and I set out to honour its 50th Anniversary with 50 unforgettable shows. That vision now looks more like 80 shows! My last tour of Australia and New Zealand was nothing short of electrifying, and I know the April tour will raise the bar even higher. I’m counting down the days until JBLZE share our final powerful, celebration of Physical Graffiti together.”

The landmark tour will let fans experience classics such as “Kashmir” and “The Wanton Song” live alongside deeper cuts like “In The Light” and “Boogie With Stu.” Along with playing the iconic album in its entirety, other Led Zeppelin favourites including “Good Times Bad Times,” “Whole Lotta Love” and of course “Stairway To Heaven” find their way into the setlist.
“For me, this isn’t just an album—it’s the album,” declares Jason Bonham.

I’m counting down the days until JBLZE share our final powerful, celebration of Physical Graffiti together.”

events.id: Fashion Holiday Programme - Upcycled Tote/Patch Workshop-110 Lambton Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Fashion Holiday Programme - Upcycled Tote/Patch Workshop
events.venue: 110 Lambton Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us for a full-day experience at Whitecliffe’s Fashion + Sustainability spaces, where Year 11–13 students will get creative through upcycling and design. In this hands-on workshop, you’ll learn to use a sewing machine and craft your own fashion accessory—either a tote bag or a custom patch—using scrap materials and pre-loved garments.
You’ll explore sustainable fashion practices, discover the creative potential of upcycling, and gain practical skills in a supportive studio environment. Led by expert tutors and supported by current students, this session is all about having fun, experimenting with fabric, and thinking differently about fashion.
Whether you’re a future designer or just curious about creative pathways, this is your chance to experience what it’s like to study Fashion at Whitecliffe.
Spots are limited—sign up now to secure your place.

events.long_description:
Join NZSO Animateur Chris Lam Sam and an ensemble of New Zealand Symphony Orchestra musicians for these free, fun and interactive concerts of music and storytelling for the whole whānau!
Enjoy musical performances of beloved Kiwi picture books - The Little Yellow Digger by Betty and Alan Gilderdale and The Bomb by Sacha Cotter and Josh Morgan, followed by an opportunity to meet the musicians at the end of the performance.
These NZSO Storytime events run for approximately 40 minutes and are recommended for preschoolers and primary school-aged tamariki, along with their caregivers.

Wednesday 15 April
- 10.30am – 11.30am
- 1.30pm – 2.30pm

As part of our March theme: Air – Breathe Again, we’re inviting you to slow down, reset, and take a moment just for you.
Join us for a free, gentle yoga and breathing session led by Emma from @breathmoveyou — designed especially with mums in mind.

Please bring a yoga mat if you have one (we’ll have a few extras).

Free event – RSVP to let us know you’re coming!

events.id: Botanical Insights talk - Women in Botany-Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Botanical Insights talk - Women in Botany
events.venue: Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
In this talk the accomplishments of a selection of brilliant, fiercely independent-minded, women botanists of the 18th, 19th and 20th centuries will be reviewed. Their work had far-reaching benefits on, amongst other things, the economic development of their countries, conservation management and higher education. One of the ‘Sisterhood’ received the Nobel Prize. Others gained high office in various learned societies and institutions. Several New Zealand botanists will be highlighted.
Located in the Treehouse Seminar Room, Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā.
Free of charge. You’re welcome to bring along your own ‘brown bag’ lunch to enjoy during the talk.
The speaker is Reg Harris, a volunteer guide [botany] at Wellington Botanic Garden.
Reg delivers botany lectures for diverse audiences, delving into plant interactions, adaptive radiation, tropisms, and more. As a dedicated volunteer guide at Wellington Botanic Garden (WBG), he pioneered collaborations between WBG and Victoria University, organising annual field labs for biology students.

events.id: Jacinda Ardern — A Different Kind of Power-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Jacinda Ardern — A Different Kind of Power
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Verb Wellington welcomes Jacinda Ardern in discussion with award-winning author Emily Perkins at the St James Theatre. As New Zealand’s youngest prime minister in over 150 years, Jacinda took a people-first approach to policy change. She commanded worldwide respect for her empathetic leadership, and changed assumptions about what a global leader can be — all while being a first-time mum. 
In her remarkable memoir, A Different Kind of Power, Jacinda shares stories from her past that shaped the future decision-making that would guide New Zealand through some of the most unprecedented challenges that our country has faced. 
Come along and hear a former prime minister tell her story, in her own words. She offers leadership and inspiration in a world where it can be hard to lead from a place of compassion.

events.id: OPENING - The Things Live Under The Bed-99C Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: OPENING - The Things Live Under The Bed
events.venue: 99C Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Opening this Wednesday the 15th of April at 5:30pm, ‘The Things Live Under The Bed’ is a solo exhibition by artist Jay Juhye Kang.
Running until the 3rd of June, don’t miss out on this vibrant and joyous new show!
———————————-
The Things Live Under The Bed is an installation exploring the ‘in-between’ fragments inhabiting our domestic spaces. Grounded in Jay’s investigation of incompleteness, the exhibition reflects on the persistent emotional residues—comfort, fear, and flickering memories—that linger within our homes.
———————-
Stunning poster by Ela Kalman

events.id: NZCFS Wellington Branch AGM-16 Mein St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: NZCFS Wellington Branch AGM
events.venue: 16 Mein St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Wellington Branch AGM for 2026 will be held on 15 April 2026. We’re pleased to announce that our Guest Speaker at the AGM is Richard Pollock, Chief Policy Advisor, New Zealand Māori Tourism.
Richard will talk about his knowledge, interest in, and engagement with China, including:
• Support for Māori business interests in the China market,
• Remarks and key highlights from four years living in Suzhou, Jiangsu province, China 2017–2021, and
• Some reflections on his role as a member of the NZ China Council and supporting outbound Chinese tourism to New Zealand.
Richard's presentation will be followed by a shared Chinese meal at $20 per head. If you would like to join us for a meal, please RSVP to wgtnzcfs@gmail.com by Friday 10 April, state number of people attending, and pay $20 to our account: 11-7200-0080836-11, account name: NZ China Friendship Society for the AGM meal.
PLEASE NOTE that to vote at the AGM or to nominate any person to the Committee or to one of the Officer positions on the Committee you must be a current financial (paid-up) member of the Society. If you are not already but wish to become a current financial member of the Society, please email the Wellington Branch Secretary at wgtnzcfs@gmail.com for further information.

events.id: The Weed Eaters - Big Forking Tour Q&A screening (Penthouse Cinema, Wellington)-205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: The Weed Eaters - Big Forking Tour Q&A screening (Penthouse Cinema, Wellington)
events.venue: 205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Be among the first to see THE WEED EATERS, the darkly comic, award-winning film already hailed as "the most fun I’ve had at the movies in a long time” (The Spinoff).
In this highlight of last year's NZ International Film Festival, four millennial slackers on a secluded New Year’s getaway stumble across their reclusive host’s long forgotten stash of ancient cannabis - and their holiday high takes a nightmare turn into accidental cannibalism…
Soon the unsuspecting stoners’ friendships, survival skills, and dietary preferences are put to the test in a film that wickedly combines the deadly and the deadpan.
As part of THE BIG FORKING TOUR of Aotearoa, we’ll be showing a special advance screening of the film, followed by a live Q&A with the creators and cast - hosted by Harry Evans, President of the Wellington Film Society.
It promises to be a memorable night with prizes, surprises and a 6-foot fork! Book now - and see it with some of your best buds.
THE WEED EATERS is Classified R13 Violence, offensive language, drug use & content that may disturb.

events.name: Weds April-15th Discruption GLOW #40 @ Berhampore (mix of short and long tees)
events.venue: 570 Adelaide Rd, Berhampore, Wellington 6023, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Looks like Wednesday might be the best weather we get this week!
Standard format of 3x around the front 6 holes & a mix of short and long teepads:
Round 1: All shorts tees
Round 2: Long tees for holes 1, 3,4,5 & 6
Round 3: All short tess
A U-Disc league event will be created for you to check into and keep track of your score.
The U-Disc layout will also direct you to teepad locations if in doubt.
Teepad lighting will be provided the short tees as these are the greater tripping hazard in the dark.
Please bring your own glow discs and torches - although you are welcome to borrow from our pool discs (box on teepad#1). A Koha jar is be there if you would like to help with our disc and lighting expenses.

events.id: DJ QBert (USA) + Alphabethead (NZ)-17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: DJ QBert (USA) + Alphabethead (NZ)
events.venue: 17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
DJ QBert (USA) + Alphabethead (NZ)
Two titans of the turntables. One night only.
Three-time DMC World Champion DJ QBert is widely regarded as one of the greatest and most influential DJs of all time. His dominance at the DMC World Championships in the early ’90s redefined what was possible with two turntables and he is widely credited with transforming the turntable from a device used to play records into a respected, expressive musical instrument. Decades later, his innovation, technical mastery and cultural impact remain unmatched. Seeing QBert live is not just a show, it is witnessing the evolution of hip hop craft in real time.
Sharing the bill is Aotearoa’s own 2002 New Zealand DMC Champion Alphabethead, a shining star of experimental hip hop and razor-sharp turntablism. "With a career spanning international tours, acclaimed projects both solo and in bands Bad Taste and The All Seeing Hand, Alphabethead represents the very best of New Zealand DJ culture.
This is a rare meeting of eras, styles and legacies at the highest level of turntablism. Miss it, and you will be hearing about it for years to come.

events.id: The Old Cold Hearts Club-12 Cambridge Terrace, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: The Old Cold Hearts Club
events.venue: 12 Cambridge Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Everyone has a secret to reveal… and Detective Raven McAllister is determined to uncover the truth.
Set over one unforgettable night at a local karaoke bar, The Old Cold Hearts Club follows Detective McAllister as he observes a wild and intriguing cast of characters. As the songs roll on and secrets begin to surface, the detective inches closer to a revelation that will throw everyone - including himself - for a loop.
Presented by Silver Noodle Soup, this original production is created and performed by young disabled artists from Te Whanganui-a-Tara. SNS is a Wellington-based charitable trust where disabled people aged approximately 16–35 are fully included in the leadership and direction of all aspects of theatrical and cinematic creativity - from development and production to promotion and performance.
Guided by experienced theatre-makers Stevie Hancox-Monk, Madeline McNamara and Hannah Kelly, the ensemble brings humour, heart and mystery to the stage in a celebration of inclusive theatre-making.
Silver Noodle Soup has been creating original performance and film work across Wellington for several years, including opening Disability Pride Week in 2019 with a performance and film showcase at BATS Theatre.
Join us at Hannah Playhouse for a night of karaoke, secrets and surprises - where nothing is quite what it seems.
Cold Noodle Soup is dedicated to making this show accessible to all audience members, if you would like to contact them directly with questions or to discuss the show please do so at silvernoodlesoup@gmail.com.
For more details about access at The Hannah please visit their website here.

events.id: 150 Years of Rugby - Wellington College-Centennial Memorial, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: 150 Years of Rugby - Wellington College
events.venue: Centennial Memorial, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
150 Years Since the First Intercollegiate Rugby Match – Nelson Old Boys, This Is Ours Too!
In April 2026, Wellington College and Nelson College will come together to celebrate 150 years since the first intercollegiate rugby match in the world — a milestone in the proud history of both schools.
As part of the celebrations in Wellington, we want to make sure Nelson College is strongly represented — and that starts with our own event:
𝗡𝗲𝗹𝘀𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲 𝗢𝗹𝗱 𝗕𝗼𝘆𝘀’ 𝗔𝘀𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 – 𝗪𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴𝘁𝗼𝗻 𝗕𝗿𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗵 𝗙𝘂𝗻𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻
Backbencher GastroPub, Wellington
Friday 17 April 2026
5.00pm – 9.00pm
Hosted by the Nelson College Old Boys' Association Wellington Branch, this dedicated Nelson College gathering is a fantastic opportunity to reconnect, share stories, and support our 1st XV ahead of the big match.
Bring your old team. Rally your year group. Spread the word.
These milestone weekends are about more than rugby — they’re about camaraderie and celebrating what it means to be Nelson College men.
We welcome all Nelson College supporters, parents, Old Boys, partners and Nelson College Old Girls!
Cost: Free
(Registration encouraged for catering purposes.)
Register here: https://community.nelson.school.nz/WCRugby150
𝗢𝗳𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝟭𝟱𝟬𝘁𝗵 𝗖𝗲𝗹𝗲𝗯𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 – 𝗛𝗼𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝗯𝘆 𝗪𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴𝘁𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲
Wellington College is hosting a superb three-day programme of events across 16–18 April 2026, and Old Boys are warmly encouraged to take part:
150th Anniversary Golf Day
Thursday 16 April 2026
A premium team-of-four event including lunch, dinner, and competitions. Play alongside rugby greats such as Christian Cullen, Ian Kirkpatrick, Victor Vito, and Jason Eaton.
Register / Purchase tickets: https://www.sporty.co.nz/viewform/423419
Rugby Legends Lunch
Friday 17 April 2026
An afternoon of lunch, beverages, entertainment, and exclusive auction moments. Hear from:
Marc Ellis
Dane Coles
Sir Graham Henry
Register / Purchase tickets: https://www.sporty.co.nz/viewform/423419
150th Anniversary Match Day
Saturday 18 April 2026 - Kick Off 1.30pm
The historic anniversary match between Wellington College and Nelson College, played at the Wellington College grounds — a day not to be missed.
Match information & updates: https://www.wellington-college.school.nz/wcrugby150/
If you can't make it in person, SKYTV will be covering the game.
Travelling to the capital?
A big thank you to Rydges Hotels & Resorts for supporting the celebrations with a 20% accommodation discount for attendees.
Rydges Wellington City
RZWELL
https://www.rydges.com/.../wellington-college-150th.../
Rydges Wellington Airport
RZWEAP
https://www.rydges.com/.../wellington-college-150th.../
Let’s show up in numbers.
Let’s make some noise.

events.id: IT Holiday Programme - Explore Cyber Security and Mobile App Development-110 Lambton Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: IT Holiday Programme - Explore Cyber Security and Mobile App Development
events.venue: 110 Lambton Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us for an exciting and interactive session designed for curious minds who want to explore the world of technology.
Please note: Students need to bring their own laptop for this activity.
In the first part of the programme, you will step into the world of Cyber Security. You’ll discover how cyber threats work, learn about common security risks that affect individuals and organisations, and experience hands-on demonstrations of how these threats can be identified and prevented. This session will give you practical insights into how digital systems are protected in today’s connected world.
In the second part, you will become an app developer for the day. You’ll learn the basics of mobile application development and create a simple mobile app step by step. The best part? You will be able to test the app on your own mobile phone and see your creation come to life.
This session is designed to be interactive, practical, and fun, giving you a taste of two exciting areas in the tech industry: Cyber Security and Mobile App Development.
Whether you are interested in technology, curious about how apps are built, or want to understand how digital systems stay secure, this session will give you a great introduction to the world of IT.
No prior coding experience required — just bring your curiosity!
Free to attend. Spaces are limited - register now!

events.id: Magic and Revolution with Claire Mabey-Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Magic and Revolution with Claire Mabey
events.venue: Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join wielder of words and writer of revolutions Claire Mabey at Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Central Library for this celebration of her new book The Raven’s Eye Rebellion. Come along to meet the author, hear her weave a story from the pages of her new book, ask her your burning questions, and pick up a copy from our friends at Unity Books while you’re at it. You could even get it signed while you’re here!
When writing was first invented several thousand years ago, many regarded it as a form of magic. Claire’s words call this magic to life in a world of fantasy, books, intrigue, one-eyed ravens, and trees that sleep (or don’t). Will you be susceptible to her spell?
This event is suitable for readers aged 8-15, or anyone keen for a good fantasy read. Rangatahi under the age of 14 must be accompanied by a caregiver.
Many thanks to Unity Books for their generous support of this event.
Nau mai rā tatou katoa | Everybody is welcome

events.id: Joanne McNally — Pinotphile-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Joanne McNally — Pinotphile
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
She’s a comedian boasting stage, television, and podcast success. Joanne McNally heads to The Opera House in Wellington for a new stage show. Pinotphile displays the Irish comic’s wry wit and sense of the absurd. Hailed by Variety as One to Watch, McNally has been keeping herself busy since her last tour by ruining men’s lives (and her own). For Pinophile, she’s still single, still unfertilised, and laden with irreverent, ridiculous and hilarious war stories. Join Joanne for a wild ride as she navigates situationships, revenge, frenemies and rejection. The Irish Examiner describes her trademark style as ‘joyously nuts’. 
You may have come across Joanne as a co-host of the award-winning podcast My Therapist Ghosted Me. She’s also had riotous appearances on Taskmaster, The Jonathan Ross Show and The Big Fat Quiz of Everything.

Free entry!

events.name: Tarot Reading with Arbor Tarot
events.venue: Garibaldi Club, 118 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
We look forward to hosting tarot readings in the shop every Thursday, between 5pm and 7pm. Nina from Arbor Tarot describes it thus:
‘Tarot is an ancient language in code. For many, reading the Tarot means predicting the future. For a tarologist, it means turning the pages of the great book of consciousness and exploring the mysteries of the soul.
‘As a scholar of ancient Greek and Latin, I explore Tarot's intricate symbology and archetypes, drawing from mythology, astrology, alchemy, Christianity, Kabbalah, and Hermeticism. As a translator of Tarot language, I help you connect directly with your innermost Voice, supporting you as you move forward across all areas of your life. By making choices aligned to your true nature, you will not be a passive spectator but an active and conscious creator of your destiny.’
Arbor Tarot (instagram: @arbor_tarot)
Full reading: $40
3 cards: $25

events.id: Flight of the Conchords Wellington | Night 1-92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Flight of the Conchords Wellington | Night 1
events.venue: 92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The New Zealand warm-up shows precede Bret and Jemaine’s three Southern California appearances in May: Thursday 7th May at the Santa Barbara Bowl, and their two Sold-Out appearances at the 2026 edition of the Netflix Is A Joke Festival at The Greek Theatre in Los Angeles on Saturday 9th May, and Sunday 10th May. These are the Flight of the Conchords' first performances in 10 years.
New Zealanders Bret McKenzie and Jemaine Clement debuted on HBO in 2005 in an edition of the comedy series “One-Night Stand,” returning to the network in 2007 for the debut season of their acclaimed, Emmy-nominated series “Flight of the Conchords,” which followed fictionalized versions of their lives in New York City.
The duo also signed a worldwide deal with Sub Pop Records for their recorded music, going on to release the Grammy-winning EP, The Distant Future in 2007, followed by two full-lengths and a live album - their self-titled debut in 2008, I Told You I Was Freaky in 2009, and Live In London in 2019. They also headlined in the US and the UK multiple times, including their wildly successful “Flight of the Conchords Sing Flight of the Conchords Tour” in 2016.
The Conchords’ have also enjoyed individual success. Clement is the co-creator (and occasional guest star) of the comedy horror TV shows Wellington Paranormal and What We Do In The Shadows. He had a recurring role in FX’s Legion, and appeared in films like Men In Black 3, The Minecraft Movie, Megan 2.0, and most recently as “Dr. Garvin” in the last two Avatar films (The Way of Water in 2022; and Fire and Ash in 2025).
McKenzie was the songwriter for The Muppets, which won him an Academy Award® for Best Original Song for “Man or Muppet,” and “Muppets Most Wanted.” He's also written songs for TV and film, including The Simpsons and The Minecraft Movie. Bret also recently released two solo albums for Sub Pop - Songs Without Jokes in 2022 and Freak Out City in 2024, and has been touring live in New Zealand, Australia, the United States, and the United Kingdom.

New Zealanders Bret McKenzie and Jemaine Clement debuted on HBO in 2005 in an edition of the comedy series “One-Night Stand,” returning to the network in 2007 for the debut season of their acclaimed, Emmy-nominated series “Flight of the Conchords,” which followed fictionalized versions of their lives in New York City.
The duo also signed a worldwide deal with Sub Pop Records for their recorded music, going on to release the Grammy-winning EP, The Distant Future in 2007, followed by two full-lengths and a live album - their self-titled debut in 2008, I Told You I Was Freaky in 2009, and Live In London in 2019.

events.venue: Wellington
events.long_description:
A lively musical retelling of The Tortoise and the Hare with a quintet of New Zealand Symphony Orchestra musicians. Sing, move, and watch the story unfold through music and illustration!
The New Zealand Symphony Orchestra (NZSO) brings Aesop’s classic fable The Tortoise and the Hare to life in this playful interactive concert for young audiences aged 4-7.
Led by Chris Lam Sam and a quintet of NZSO musicians, the story unfolds through live music, narration, and new illustrations by Stephen Templer.
In this concert you will:
Hear live music from NZSO musicians
Help tell the story through singing and movement
Watch the story unfold through illustrations
Meet the musicians after the concert
When | Āhea
Fri 17 Apr 2026, 10.30–11.30am
Fri 17 Apr 2026, 12.30–1.30pm

Sat 18 Apr 2026, 12.30–1.30pm
Cost | Te utu
0–2s: Free
Child: $2
Adult: $5
Children must be accompanied by an adult
Presented in collaboration with Te Papa and made possible through funding from the Freemasons Lankhuyzen/Whetu-Kairangi Masonic Trust.

events.long_description:
Respawn Solo Build Showdown – School Holiday Fortnite Event
Think you’ve got what it takes to secure the Victory Royale? Join us at Respawn Esports Center for the Respawn Solo Build Showdown, a Fortnite Battle Royale tournament during the school holidays where players will compete in solo matches with builds enabled.
Location: Respawn Esports Centre – 9 Manners Street, Wellington
Date: Friday 17th April
Event Schedule
• Check-in opens: 11:00 AM
• Games begin: 12:00 PM sharp
Tournament Format
Players will compete in multiple Fortnite Solo Battle Royale matches with builds enabled. Points are awarded each round based on placement and eliminations, with scores accumulating across all matches.
The players with the highest total points at the end of the qualifiers will entered into the finals, for the winner to take all!
What to Expect
• Multiple Fortnite Solo Battle Royale matches
• Points for eliminations and final placement
• Leaderboard tracking throughout the event
• Friendly but competitive environment
• Meet other Fortnite players in the local community
Entry Cost: $10
Spaces may be limited depending on the number of available gaming stations, so keep an eye out for the registration link to secure your spot.
Capped out at 46 players, first 46 players signed up will be playing so get in early on the day!
Start practising those builds — we’ll see you in the Battle Bus!

events.id: Lost Wax in Three - Hard Wax - Friday Nights-65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Lost Wax in Three - Hard Wax - Friday Nights
events.venue: 65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Over three sessions, on 17 April, 24 April and 8 May, 5.30pm - 8.30pm, create stunning jewellery using the age-old lost wax technique. This is an excellent opportunity to treat yourself to some creative fun, or have a date night with a friend!
In weeks one and two you'll learn how to work with jeweller's hard wax to create beautiful textures in metal that can be used to create amazing rings or objects. Your waxes will be sent away for casting in the metal of your choice.
Then there will be a week's break before the third session, to allow time for everyone's waxes to be cast. The third session covers how to trim, clean and finish your casts, turning them into original jewellery ready to wear!
More advanced students can use these sessions to complete projects in a class setting.
Included in the class fee are all your different types of waxes, sandpaper and courier costs.
Not included are the casting and metal costs which vary from student to student depending on what sized ring they want to make and the type of metal you choose. Small castings can cost around $6.65 - $12 and might cost between $7 to $30 in metals depending on if you use red bronze, white bronze or silver.
This class is great for teens and kids over 12 or so. Anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.
Workshops' fee $256, early bird $230 till 22 March.

events.id: Flight of the Conchords Wellington | Night 2-92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Flight of the Conchords Wellington | Night 2
events.venue: 92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The New Zealand warm-up shows precede Bret and Jemaine’s three Southern California appearances in May: Thursday 7th May at the Santa Barbara Bowl, and their two Sold-Out appearances at the 2026 edition of the Netflix Is A Joke Festival at The Greek Theatre in Los Angeles on Saturday 9th May, and Sunday 10th May. These are the Flight of the Conchords' first performances in 10 years.
New Zealanders Bret McKenzie and Jemaine Clement debuted on HBO in 2005 in an edition of the comedy series “One-Night Stand,” returning to the network in 2007 for the debut season of their acclaimed, Emmy-nominated series “Flight of the Conchords,” which followed fictionalized versions of their lives in New York City.
The duo also signed a worldwide deal with Sub Pop Records for their recorded music, going on to release the Grammy-winning EP, The Distant Future in 2007, followed by two full-lengths and a live album - their self-titled debut in 2008, I Told You I Was Freaky in 2009, and Live In London in 2019. They also headlined in the US and the UK multiple times, including their wildly successful “Flight of the Conchords Sing Flight of the Conchords Tour” in 2016.
The Conchords’ have also enjoyed individual success. Clement is the co-creator (and occasional guest star) of the comedy horror TV shows Wellington Paranormal and What We Do In The Shadows. He had a recurring role in FX’s Legion, and appeared in films like Men In Black 3, The Minecraft Movie, Megan 2.0, and most recently as “Dr. Garvin” in the last two Avatar films (The Way of Water in 2022; and Fire and Ash in 2025).
McKenzie was the songwriter for The Muppets, which won him an Academy Award® for Best Original Song for “Man or Muppet,” and “Muppets Most Wanted.” He's also written songs for TV and film, including The Simpsons and The Minecraft Movie. Bret also recently released two solo albums for Sub Pop - Songs Without Jokes in 2022 and Freak Out City in 2024, and has been touring live in New Zealand, Australia, the United States, and the United Kingdom.

events.id: SIKA Sound Journey - WELLINGTON-293 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: SIKA Sound Journey - WELLINGTON
events.venue: 293 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
SIKA Sound Journey - 'Between the Worlds'
Something between an ancient ceremony and a movie soundtrack.
Please bring a yoga mat, pillow and blanket. Be prepared to drop into a deep space.
Door sales will be available if there is space. Advanced booking is recommended.
Between the Worlds is an evening Sound Journey shaped by over thirty years of deep ceremonial and immersive sound practice. Blending primal rhythm, atmospheric voice, deep didgeridoo, and field recordings, the journey unfolds organically — shifting from dynamic, embodied energy into vast, cinematic stillness.
This is not passive listening.
It is an experience to be entered.
As sound layers, dissolve, and reform, participants are guided into a liminal space where time softens and inner landscapes awaken. The journey becomes a passage — one that listeners don’t just hear, but inhabit.
Guided by the myth of the Wandering Forest Spirit, Between the Worlds invites a reconnection with self, with nature, and with something older than language. Ceremonial, immersive, and deeply evocative, this is an evening to slow down, listen deeply, and cross into the unseen.
It's like a soundtrack that is crafted to go with your life.
Sika's Sound Journeys are truly unique. They are sonic explorations that feature didgeridoos, drums, flutes, voice and orchestral elements. A collection of foraged sounds from objects he has recorded in nature, add even more textures. Many of his sounds are completely unrecognisable and abstract. Immersed in this cocoon of abstract sound, we can find ourselves in the most surprising places in our mind. In this timeless realm, beyond the normal limitations of rational reality, anything is possible.
An epic adventure deep inside yourself.
Journey to worlds within worlds on incredible sound waves.
Sika creates a safe space to relax deeply and connect with our inner guidance. This Sound Journey begins and ends with a short guided visualisation.
There will be two journeys with an interval. Most people choose to lie down, so bring a yoga mat, pillow and blanket and get really comfortable.
A journal is also recommended.

events.name: Funky Fridays Vol. 9 - Not My Sister, Halo, Buna
events.venue: 167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
We're back after the summer break with another night of house, techno, disco, bass & more at Moon with Funky Fridays Vol. 9!
Created to revive and celebrate the underground electronic scene, Funky Friday is more than just a night out, it’s a space for music lovers, dancers, and creatives to connect through rhythm and energy. Each edition features a rotating lineup of talented DJs, both local and international, ensuring a fresh and unique experience every time.
Come with an open mind and leave with tired feet.
With a boiler room style, this edition features legends Halo, Buna and Not My Sister
$10 Early Birds, 15$ General Sales available now via UTR, or $20 on the door.
Get your tickets early before it gets sold out!

Times:
9-10 Not My Sister
10-11.30 Halo
11.30-1 Buna
1-2 Not My Sister
-------------------------------------------------------
NOT MY SISTER - HOUSE/TECH HOUSE
Hailing from Italy and now rooted in Wellington’s vibrant scene, Not My Sister quickly burst onto Aotearoa’s dance floors and has been on a rapid rise ever since. She’s lit up festivals like Twisted Frequency, Kiwiburn, Earthbeat, CubaDupa, Taniwha’s Den and Arkadien and supported international acts including KC Lights.
She currently holds a radio residency on Wellington’s iconic Radio Active, where her show dives deep into house and underground sounds.
Heavily influenced by funk and disco, and driven by a love for vocals, groove and infectious pulse of 90s House, Not My Sister delivers high-energy, uplifting sets that weave together melodic basslines with soulful, tech and deep house sounds. Expect pure passion and play from the moment she steps up to the decks.

SoundCloud: www.soundcloud.com/not-my-sister
Facebook:
www.facebook.com/notmysista

Instagram: www.instagram.com/not.my.sister
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@not.my.sister
HALO - DEEP TECH
With roots in the early 2000s, Halo brings decades of dancefloor intuition and community spirit to the party. Crafting sets that can flow through deep, melodic, techy and uplifting terrain she creates the perfect vibe for each time, each place and each crowd, always with a unique journey in mind. Her recent appearances include Flock, Yatra, Newtown Festival and CubaDupa creating magnetic dancefloors wherever she plays.
Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/halo.nz/

Instagram: halo.rakau
Mixcloud: https://www.mixcloud.com/Halo_Wellington/
BUNA - TECHNO
Buna is a local DJ and producer from Te Whanganui a Tara, and has been involved in the local dance music scene for the past couple of years, playing at the likes of Twisted Frequency and local raves/clubs. He has a love for sound system music and club culture, and you can expect a mix techno/house.

events.name: Nectax (UK) | JUNGLE NIGHT | WELLINGTON
events.venue: 38 Dixon Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Ghost Media x Rhythm Output presents: NECTAX (UK)
Friday 17th April
Support from Clova, Young Gho$t, JSwizzle, Ohimesama Breaks & Whaea G
Afters


Ghost Media and Rhythm Output are teaming up once again for a spectacular extravaganza of junglism! This time we are elated to welcome none other than NECTAX, in our opinion one of the most exciting jungle producers of modern times!
Nectax exists in the gaps; a breakbeat scientist at heart, never fraternising too heavily with any single sonic blueprint in pursuit of his heterogenous sound. He has released on some of the genre’s most sought after labels, including Over/Shadow, Future Retro, Hooversound, Up Ya Archives and DJ Flight’s Play:Musik.
On support, he will be joined by a classic cast of Poneke’s finest DJs - CLOVA and WHAEA G, both hot off the backs of legendary CubaDupa sets, and OHIMESAMA BREAKS, hot off the back of a massive set at Resonance Festival. Finally, Young Gho$t will open up proceedings, with as an of yet unnamed back to back partner. Who could it be?
Venue will be announced soon… in the meantime score some cheap tix and lock in for a night of serious jungle, breakbeat and hardcore!

Join us for a behind-the-scenes tour of Wellington's iconic St James Theatre and The Opera House. Discover hidden stories, architectural splendour, and rich theatrical history as you step behind the velvet curtain and explore two of the city's most beloved performance spaces. Led by expert guides, this tour takes you on a journey through over a century of theatre magic. Tread the boards in the footsteps of legendary performers. Uncover the secrets of stagecraft and marvel at the breathtaking details of these heritage-listed buildings. From opulent foyers and ornate auditoriums to backstage corridors brimming with history, you'll see the theatres as you've never seen them before.

events.id: Irish Set Dancing - day of dance-83 Upland Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Irish Set Dancing - day of dance
events.venue: 83 Upland Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Does Irish music get your feet tapping? This is your opportunity to get up and dance to some cracking tunes.
On Saturday 18th of April will be a full day of Set Dancing workshops, wrapped up by an evening Céilí. Come to St Michael’s Hall in Kelburn to meet new people and enjoy a great time in a friendly and welcoming atmosphere. No dancing experience needed, just a bit of coordination, some energy, and a big sense of fun!
Set dances are the traditional social dances of Ireland, and are so much fun to dance. Have a look here to see what it's all about: https://www.facebook.com/reel/1062173322541491.
No experience necessary, suitable for beginners and experienced alike. This is not Irish step dancing, not Riverdance!
Experienced teacher Helen Stonehouse will guide you through the moves and figures.
For many years she has taught Set Dancing at workshops and festivals across the UK, Australia, and Aotearoa. Our amazing Round The House Céilí Band, featuring Roger Stonehouse, Em Griffiths, and Thomas Stonehouse, will be playing lively jigs and reels and polkas for us throughout the day.
There are two workshops during the day. In the evening we’ll have a Céilí when we'll dance some of what you learnt in the workshops plus some Irish céilí dances.
Anyone is welcome to buy tickets to the Céilí without attending the workshops.
Schedule:
Workshops 10am - 4.30pm
Céilí 7.30 - 10:30pm
Handy information:
You can come along without a partner, there will always be someone to dance with
Age 10 upwards. Under 16s accompanied by an adult
We recommend smooth-soled shoes if you have them
Bring a water bottle, hydration is key!
Parking is limited, we recommend catching the bus. Routes 4, 18, and 21 all stop right next to the hall.
If you’re unwell then please stay home this time, we’ll see you at the next one
Door sales may be available on the day (cash only):
All day and evening: General Admission $80, unwaged $55
Daytime workshops only: General Admission $65, unwaged $45
Evening Céilí only: General Admission $30, unwaged $20
Pro tip - online tickets will be cheaper!

events.id: Make Your Own Robot at Old St Paul's-Old St Paul's Cathedral, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Make Your Own Robot at Old St Paul's
events.venue: Old St Paul's Cathedral, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
What does a robot look like to you? Your dog, Kākāpō? Humanoid like your grandmother, Meryl? Or more industrial like your dad’s vacuum cleaner? We’re putting on an art class for human children like you, and over two fun-filled hours you can create your very own robot!
Robots have been around for a long time, since the 1940s in fact, when American-born British robotics pioneer William Grey Walter built two robots called Elmer and Elsie out of old war materials and alarm clocks. They looked a bit like tortoises, and they moved by themselves in response to light.
Closer to home, in Aotearoa New Zealand, Eric Cummins from Rangiora has been making real working robots out of his garage since 1975. Some of the robots Eric designed now live in the Christchurch Museum of Toys & Collectibles.
In this art class you can manufacture a robot of your own design. We’ll provide all the materials, like boxes, aluminium foil, bottle caps, tubing, glue and so on, but you’ll need to bring along a real adult.
You’ll be creating robots for the future while appreciating the past in one of Te Whanganui-a-Tara’s most famously beautiful buildings – constructed by humans 160 years ago and now cared for by Heritage New Zealand Pouhere Taonga.
For ages 6-12.
All materials will be provided.

events.description:

events.id: Self Defence Foundations - 2hr Introduction-160 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Self Defence Foundations - 2hr Introduction
events.venue: 160 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Being prepared is smart. Come be prepared with protective and self-defence skills that will last a lifetime.
Imagine enjoying the peace and freedom of no longer feeling caught without options in threatening and unsafe situations, for the rest of your life. Whether working day to day, going on travels, or simply talking to your kids about their safety, you will be armed with knowledge, tactics, techniques, physical conditioning, muscle memory and a sharpened sense of awareness from mental conditioning, that you can take with you where ever you go. Your Krav training will become like a ‘practical toolkit for protection’ that will accompany you for the rest of your life.
Freedom doesn’t come free though, as all freedom is paid for by sacrifice. In this case you will be sacrificing a wee bit of comfort and ease, by being willing to train hard once (or twice) a week for a term or more, to earn that freedom which will become a part of you. It is an investment into your peace and safety for the future. Giving up is never the answer if you need to survive a danger situation, and the same principle applies if you want to gain something truly of value: an upgrade of who you are with more peace and freedom that will last a lifetime.
If this sounds like something you would like to explore, we invite you to our 2hr Intro Day on Sat April 18th, 10am-12pm, Mt Cook School Hall, 160 Tory Street $20 for a 2hr introductory class just for new people to try a class, meet the instructors, see how we train, learn some basics, and see what Krav can do for you. There will be time for Q & A, membership info, safety…etc. You can then decide whether to fully join us for Term 2 classes, which will start the week after.
Date: Sat April 18th
Time: 10am - 12pm
Location: Mt Cook School Hall 160 Tory Street
Cost: $20 at door
Bring: Active Wear and Water
Info: www.tacticalsurvival.co.nz
Reserve Spot via email: contact@tacticalsurvival.co.nz
#TacticalSurvival #Krav #Preparedness

events.id: Three-Region Savory Dumplings Combo | Bánh ba Miền-196 Newlands Road, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Three-Region Savory Dumplings Combo | Bánh ba Miền
events.venue: 196 Newlands Road, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us for a hands-on Vietnamese cooking workshop where we’ll prepare a Three Region Savory Dumplings Combo together, a delicious journey through flavours and techniques from different parts of Vietnam.


In this session, you’ll learn how to make:


* Steamed pork rice pyramids
* Clear shrimp and pork dumplings
* Sticky rice dumplings
Guided step by step, we’ll cook side by side, share tips, and enjoy the process in a relaxed, welcoming environment. This workshop is suitable for beginners and those who simply enjoy cooking and eating together as a community.


This class is delivered in collaboration with I Love Viet Food, and made possible thanks to the generous support and sponsorship.
Your participation helps us continue running meaningful, accessible community workshops and creating more opportunities to cook, learn, and connect together.
We look forward to welcoming you into the kitchen.
Ticket Information
• $10 per person – appreciation contribution towards facilitator effort
• Optional koha/donation welcome to support VHNZ Cuisine community programmes

events.id: Wellington Women's Lifestyle Expo 2026--41.285826, 174.778333-Facebook
events.name: Wellington Women's Lifestyle Expo 2026
events.long_description:
Date: Sat 18 & Sun 19 April, 2026
Venue: TSB Arena, Wellington
Opening hours: 10am - 4pm
Tickets: https://bit.ly/4arW30t
The ultimate girls' day out awaits you, filled with shopping, pampering and mouth-watering indulgences, and the chance to win amazing prizes!
Gather your besties and prepare for an unforgettable day at the Wellington Women’s Lifestyle Expo on Sat 18 & Sun 19 April at TSB Arena!
150 COMPANIES
The expo will have something for everyone, featuring over 130 of the best lifestyle companies under one roof, including gourmet food and beverages, fashion and beauty, jewellery, health and wellness, unique gifts, art, homeware, eco-products and much more. It’s the perfect opportunity for a girls’ day out, or to treat yourself to a day leisurely wandering between exhibitors.
GOODIE BAGS
Pre-purchase here: https://bit.ly/4arW30t
Our mystery goodie bags are $20 each and usually include a variety of beauty, skincare, snacks, lifestyle products and more. The contents can vary from bag to bag, adding to the mystery! Available to purchase online with your tickets until the Friday night before the expo, after that (on event weekend) you will only be able to purchase goodie bags at the expo. We recommend pre-purchasing as they often sell out.
TASTE ZONE
Appreciating that shopping, pampering, trying and testing can be hard work, the expo Taste Zone (a mini food show) will be on hand to offer pick-me-ups, tasty treats and bites to keep you going.
TICKETS
Pre-purchase here: https://bit.ly/4arW30t
Online Pre-Sales (available until Fri 17 Apr):
Earlybird x1 - $9
Earlybird x2 - $16
Child x1 (aged 6-12) - $5
Kids 5 & Under Free
Please note, there will be a small booking fee for online purchases.
DOOR SALES
Adult x1 - $13
Child x1 (6-12) - $6
Kids 5 & Under Free
EXHIBITORS
Keen to exhibit at the expo? Find out more at www.womenslifestyleexpo.co.nz/apply, or call the friendly NZME Events team on 06 354 0498, or email enquire@events.nzme.co.nz
FIND OUT MORE:

events.name: MOVEMENT (FIRST EDITION)
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
A new club night for Wellington. Nostalgic modernism for retro-futurists.
Alternative sounds of the eighties and beyond to make you move.
Your host is DJ Matai, known for sets spanning vintage goth, new wave and post-punk. Expect to hear the classic and the obscure. Not only a selector, but a genuine alternative music fanatic, he is also known for running Razored Raw and drumming in bands such as No Sector and Maced.
Joined by guests:
DJ Idle Hands- Katie is a vinyl DJ who has been playing around Pōneke for more than a decade. She is the drummer in DÄHTM, works in film sound, and is the former host of the Dolls House radio show on the (now defunct) Fast Radio. Expect 80s punk, cold wave, and some Continental deep cuts.
DJ Boss Dude- It's your ol' mate, thee space vampire of Newtown, thee headhoncho of Creeps record parlour, thee master of many genres, 80s electronique, synthesizer, Italo-disco, and New Wave.
$15 door/ Presales via UTR
8pm-late

events.name: Respawn Valorant 5v5 Tournament
events.venue: 9 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Respawn Valorant 5v5 is a full-day competitive event at Respawn Esports Centre featuring an 8-team Double Elimination bracket, where teams must lose twice before being knocked out.
https://ggcircuit.tourneybit.com/.../48332977.../respawn-5v5
DATE & TIME
Date: Saturday, April 18th
Check-in: 10:00 AM NZT (Strict cut-off at 10:45 AM)
Matches Start: 11:00 AM NZT
VENUE
Respawn Esports Centre
9 Manners Street, Wellington
FORMAT
Stage 1: Double Elimination Bracket (8 Teams)
All teams start in the Upper Bracket
Lose once → drop to Lower Bracket
Lose twice → eliminated
Bracket continues until 2 teams remain
Match Format:
Early rounds: Best-of-1 (Bo1)
Upper Bracket Final: Best-of-3 (Bo3)
Lower Bracket Final: Best-of-3 (Bo3)
Stage 2: Grand Final
Upper Bracket Winner vs Lower Bracket Winner
Best-of-3 (Bo3)
(Optional: bracket reset if UB team loses — include only if you have time)
Seeding
Random seeding (or based on sign-up order / prior results if applicable)
REGISTRATION & FEES

Price: $15 per person - $75 per team
Team Size: 5v5 (1 substitute allowed)
Capacity: 8 teams
Registration closes: April 18th at 10:45 AM
RULES & CONDUCT
Peripherals: BYO keyboard, mouse, and headset recommended
Accounts: Players must use their own Riot accounts
Punctuality: 5-minute no-show rule = match forfeit
Conduct: Toxicity, cheating, or griefing results in instant disqualification
PRIZES

Prize pool made up of in-store credit + sponsor rewards (based on entries)
Redeemable for game time, food, drinks, merch, and future events

events.id: Nervous System Support-175 Vivian Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Nervous System Support
events.venue: 175 Vivian Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Nervous System Support is a gentle, practical introduction to understanding and working with your body’s stress response.
In this 60-minute experiential workshop, you’ll learn simple, science-informed tools to help your nervous system settle and feel safer in everyday life. Together we’ll explore how stress shows up in the body, why “calming down” isn’t always as easy as it sounds, and what actually helps the nervous system shift.
You’ll be guided through a series of accessible practices, including:
Orienting to your environment for safety and presence
Squeeze and release to discharge tension
Havening touch for self-soothing
The physiological sigh for rapid calming
Gentle movement to music
A guided, body-based meditation
Time to re-orient and integrate
An optional sharing circle to reflect and connect
This is not about “fixing” yourself. It’s about learning to listen to your body, build capacity, and create small moments of safety that accumulate over time.
You’ll leave with tools you can use at home, at work, or in moments of overwhelm — no special equipment or experience required.
Come as you are. Move as little or as much as feels right. You are always in choice.
It would benefit everyone's experience if you arrive on time.
Please bring:
A yoga mat or blanket for the meditation (bring any other supports that provide you with comfort or safety)
A journal and a pen
Water and a snack for after the workshop
Important note:
This workshop is designed for people who are generally resourced and able to self-ground. It is not suitable for anyone currently experiencing a manic episode, in acute crisis, or with untreated complex PTSD.
Nervous system work can sometimes surface uncomfortable sensations, emotions, or memories. This is a natural part of being human and of working with the body. For this reason, participants need to have the capacity to return to a sense of safety on their own and have support available outside the session if needed.
This event is educational and experiential in nature and is not a substitute for therapy or mental health care.

events.long_description:
Ever wondered how to strut your stuff in heels with confidence and flair?
The secret lies in mastering the technique! From flawless walks and captivating combos to strength-building exercises and perfect posture, it’s all about technique and practice.
Why is heel technique important?
- Safety First: Proper technique prevents injuries, ensuring you can dance for years to come.
- Strength & Posture: Build the muscles needed to support your ankles, knees, and back, while perfecting that elegant posture.
- Confidence Boost: Knowing you have the technique down pat gives you the confidence to own the floor!
Whether you’re a beginner or looking to refine your skills, this class will cover:
- Walks that turn heads
- Seamless combos
- Targeted exercises for strength
- Posture tips to keep you looking and feeling your best
Bookings via zoekeesueguidance.com

events.long_description:
Presented by: 100% GOOD & UTR
Born with the blues in her blood, Deva has forged a rare blend of masterful songwriting, breathtaking vocal talent, and soulful production. Her ability to convey both profound joy and deep sorrow through her lyrical and melodic compositions often leaves audiences moved to tears and overflowing with emotion.
Her career has taken her from iconic stages around the world to the big screen, including a role in David O. Russell’s acclaimed film American Hustle, alongside Christian Bale, Bradley Cooper, Jennifer Lawrence, and Robert De Niro.
Her voice is also featured on the soundtrack of The Experimenter (starring Winona Ryder and Peter Sarsgaard). She has performed at renowned venues and festivals such as the Hollywood Bowl, Carnegie Hall, Bonnaroo, and the Montreal Jazz Festival.
Now New Zealanders will have the chance to be the first in the world to celebrate FUTURE CLASSIC VOL ll: FUTURE with Deva and her band. Don’t miss the chance to catch this powerhouse up-close-and-personal in a city or town near you!

events.id: Mozart Requiem and Christopher Tin’s To Shiver the Sky-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Mozart Requiem and Christopher Tin’s To Shiver the Sky
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
From the earliest dreams of flight to one of the greatest choral masterpieces ever written — Wellington is in for a treat in April. Wellington’s Orpheus Choir joins forces with Orchestra Wellington to present a mighty programme. Mozart Requiem and Christopher Tin’s To Shiver the Sky explores five centuries of human imagination and purpose.
Mozart’s Requiem stands as one of the greatest choral works ever composed. Written in the final months of his life, it sets the words to the Catholic requiem mass with blazing dramatic power. The oratorio has a depth of feeling that has captivated audiences for more than two centuries. To Shiver the Sky is an epic choral spectacular from Grammy-winning composer Christopher Tin. It draws on texts from da Vinci, Hildegard von Bingen, Ovid, Jules Verne, Amelia Earhart and John F. Kennedy. The work builds a sweeping musical history of human flight from imagined wings to the Moon landing. Orchestra and choir conductor Brent Stewart will also lead four soloists through the concert. They include soprano Emma Pearson, alto Charlotte Secker, tenor Ridge Ponini, and bass Robert Tucker.

events.long_description:
The Wellington Women’s Lifestyle Expo brings 150 of the best lifestyle companies to TSB Arena for the ultimate girls’ weekend. 
The expo will have something for everyone. Featuring fashion, beauty, health, wellness, artisan goods, eco-products, gourmet food and beverages, and so much more. It’s the perfect opportunity for a girls’ day out or a leisurely wander between the exhibitors. 
The popular Artisan Zone will include crafters from all over New Zealand. There will be handmade products such as soaps and organic beauty goods. Jewellery, original art, home décor, and clothing. Appreciating that shopping, trying, and testing can be hard work, the expo Taste Zone will be on hand. You’ll find pick-me-ups, tasty treats and bites to keep you going.

events.id: Céilí with Round the House Céilí Band-81 Upland Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Céilí with Round the House Céilí Band
events.venue: 81 Upland Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Does Irish music get your feet tapping? This is your opportunity to get up and dance to some cracking tunes at our evening Céilí on Saturday 18th of April.
Come to St Michael’s Hall in Kelburn to meet new people and enjoy a great time in a friendly and welcoming atmosphere. No dancing experience needed, just a bit of coordination, some energy, and a big sense of fun!
Experienced caller Helen Stonehouse will guide you through all the dances. Our amazing Round The House Céilí Band, featuring Roger Stonehouse, Em Griffiths, and Thomas Stonehouse, will be playing lively jigs and reels and polkas for us.
Handy information:
- Dancing starts at 7:30pm
- Age 10 upwards. Under 16s accompanied by an adult
- Bring a water bottle, hydration is key!
- Parking is limited, we recommend catching the bus. Routes 4, 18, and 21 all stop right next to the hall.
- If you’re unwell then please stay home this time, we’ll see you at the next one
Door sales may be available on the day (cash only): General Admission $30, Unwaged $20.
Pro tip - online tickets will be cheaper!
This event is part of our Set Dance day, and there will be workshops running during the day. Set dances are the traditional social dances of Ireland, and are so much fun to dance. Have a look here to see what it's all about: https://www.facebook.com/reel/1062173322541491.
If you’re interested, head over to Humanitix to get a full day package and fill your weekend with dancing!

events.id: Hemi Hemingway ‘Wings of Desire’ Album Release Party - Te Whanganui-a-Tara-17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Hemi Hemingway ‘Wings of Desire’ Album Release Party - Te Whanganui-a-Tara
events.venue: 17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
You may have sensed rumblings or felt the winds change over the past few months. You may have noticed a quiet stirring. Well ‘Wings of Desire’, Hemi Hemingway’s sophomore album, was released into the world on 20th February through PNKSLM Recordings - with the help of Secretly Canadian Distribution. And to further celebrate the release, Hemi and his full band are coming back to Te Whanganui-a-Tara to make Meow the site of a sweaty, gothy album launch party on Saturday 18th April, before heading over to the UK/Europe for a month of shows and showcases.
Performing songs from the new album, Hemi & the Snowflakes will also be joined by special guests Dillon Jo and Mercury Fèng (solo) to help with the celebrations.
If you wanna live on the wings of desire, grab your early-bird tickets now from meow.nz!
•
HEMI HEMINGWAY
https://linktr.ee/hemihemingway
•
DILLON JO
https://dillonjomusic.bandcamp.com/
‘Dillon Jo’ is the genre defying project of Wellington Based artists Betty Smith and Georgie Levien. Dillon Jo built paths between the elements of dream pop, shoegaze, folk, alt rock, and indie pop to create their genre defying sound. Having made quite the stir in Te Whanganui-a-Tara recently, playing numerous shows around the motu.
With their catalogue steadily growing and new ideas taking shape, the band has been working on something a little more expansive on the horizon for 2026. Having already been making waves in the Wellington music scene, they believe this next chapter will be a big one, making plans for bigger and bolder live shows.
•
MERCURY FÈNG
https://xiaofeng.bandcamp.com/
Mercury Fèng (formerly Fèng) transports listeners to intimate dreamscapes by weaving together threads of yearning and introspection with shimmery synths, fuzz-laced guitars and her glassy vocals. In April 2025, MF self-released the album 'archive for humble stones', which saw the single 'Heaving' featured on the NTS Breakfast Show hosted by Flying Nun Records x Tonic.
Lucy 小凤 Beeler, the artist behind the MF persona, has previously released music as Lttle Phnx (a vowel-deficient nod to her middle name), under the now defunct cult label Crystal Magic Records. Mercury Fèng is Beeler's emergence from the ashes as an artist more adventurous and poised.

events.id: Powers / Marrowspawn / Impress-Fors, 298 Taranaki St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Powers / Marrowspawn / Impress
events.venue: Fors, 298 Taranaki St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Powers is the current incarnation of Campbell Kneale who has ping-ponged about the nether regions of interstellar sound for close to thirty earth-years aboard sonic spacecraft such as Birchville Cat Motel, Black Boned Angel, and Our Love Will Destroy the World. Known primarily as the captain of his own ship, Campbell Kneale has also collaborated and performed with some of outsider music’s most defiant and diverse experimentalists: Lee Ranaldo and Thurston Moore (Sonic Youth), Merzbow, Stephen O’Malley (Sunn O))), Keiji Haino, Borbetomagus, Phill Niblock, and Tony Conrad to name a few. Whereas the vast majority of Kneale's music revolves around the fastidious manipulation of the multitracking process, Powers pounds on the thin cracking ice of an ‘everything-all-at once’ mindset, performing and recording his immersive blitzkrieg ragas entirely in the moment.
sunsettemples.bandcamp.com campbellkneale.bandcamp.com
Marrowspawn fuses the mechanical precision of drum machine grindcore with black metal, doom and shoegaze. Described as sounding like a 'bulldozer made of hydrochloric acid'. www.marrowspawn.bandcamp.com
Impress is the moniker attributed to the mysterious sound-conjurations of Sam Curtis. What you hear is what you heard.

events.id: The Spiral Spectacular-Paekakariki-Facebook
events.name: The Spiral Spectacular
events.venue: Paekakariki
events.long_description:
Join Steam Incorporated and discover what the Central Plateau has on offer. Experience the Raurimu Spiral first hand, hauled by our beautifully restored diesel locomotive, Da 1410.
Board this special train journey at Paekakariki, Paraparaumu, Waikanae, Otaki, Levin, Palmerston North, Feilding or Marton!
Upon completion the NIMT became important to New Zealand’s economic development, and was also socially significant. The Raurimu Spiral is considered a feat of engineering ingenuity and the success of this solution contributed to the on-going effectiveness of the NIMT. Despite advances in technology and materials in the century since its completion, no feasible alternative to the Spiral has been found. Therefore, the Raurimu Spiral is a lasting tribute to Holmes’ engineering achievements and is of considerable engineering heritage importance.
Train Fare Pricing:
Adult Fare ex. Kapiti/Horowhenua & Return - $169.00
Child Fare ex. Kapiti/Horowhenua & Return - $139.00

events.description:
Join Steam Incorporated and discover what the Central Plateau has on offer.
Experience the Raurimu Spiral first hand, hauled by our beautifully restored diesel locomotive, Da 1410.
Board this special train journey at Paekakariki, Paraparaumu, Waikanae, Otaki, Levin, Palmerston North, Feilding or Marton!
Upon completion the NIMT became important to New Zealand’s economic development, and was also socially significant.
The Raurimu Spiral is considered a feat of engineering ingenuity and the success of this solution contributed to the on-going effectiveness of the NIMT.

events.id: WHAC Presents: Honest 10 on 19 Apr 2026-64 Freyberg St, Lyall Bay, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: WHAC Presents: Honest 10 on 19 Apr 2026
events.venue: 64 Freyberg St, Lyall Bay, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Hi all.
Join us at 8:30am on Sunday 19th April 2026 for a 5k or 10k run/walk over a flat course taking in Wellington's beautiful waterfront. It's a great chance to test your 5km or 10km fitness and maybe have a go at walking or running PB.
Check out other dates as well as photos and results on the WHAC website https://whac.org.nz/wp/honest-10/
EVERYONE IS WELCOME NO MATTER WHAT YOUR LEVEL OF FITNESS. The is an optional 8:15am early start for those who think they might need more time (eg over 70 mins for 10k)
[Note: If there are gales force winds, it is likely we will cancel due to safety reasons. Check back on this FB page at 7am on the morning of the race if the wind is howling as well will post any cancellation by that time. If nothing is here, then the event is going ahead
]
Organised by WHAC, the Honest 10 provides a fast, fun and friendly environment. The race course has been officially measured and there will be pacers running 40min, 45min, 50min, 55min and 60min to help you run your best.
Cost: Just $10 for 10km, $5 for 5km!!
How to enter:
1. Pre-register by email (recommended) BEFORE 5PM on Saturday 18 April. Payment methods for pre-registrations include online bank transfer (an email with your reference will be sent back to you) or pay cash on the day.
Account Name: Wellington Harrier Athletic Club
Account Number: 12-3493-0058553-00
Use "H10 [your race number]" as your reference. Eg "H10 #23"
OR
2. Sign up on the day from 8am (cash only).
Photos are free of charge and posted online (on the WHAC website) alongside results after the race. https://whac.org.nz/wp/honest-10/
Where: At the Whale's Tail next to Freyberg Pool, Oriental Bay
When: Registration from 8am. Race starts at 8:30am Sunday morning. Let us know when you register, if you request an early 8.15am start, eg if you are walking the 10km or are likely to run slower than 70mins.
***Pre-registration details***
Email the following details to honest10@whac.org.nz BEFORE 5pm Saturday 18 April.
You will receive a confirmation email back with your race number. If you select 'N' for prepaid, just bring cash to the race with you. If you select 'Y' for prepaid, pay via WHAC's bank account details below.
- Name
- Age
- 5km or 10km
- M, F or D (unfortunately we are currently unable to give age-calculated results for the gender-diverse (D) category, as our source currently only provides age-calculated result data for M and F)
- Phone number
- Prepay Y or N
BANK ACCOUNT DETAILS If you select "Y" for Prepay when you register via email, transfer your $10 entry fee (or $5 for 5k) to
Account Name: Wellington Harrier Athletic Club
Account Number: 12-3493-0058553-00
using "H10 [your race number]" as your reference. Eg "H10 #23"
Looking forward to seeing you on Sunday 19th April 2026.
