events.id: Wellington Marathon Group Runs/Course Reccie-139A Oriental Pde, Oriental Bay, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Wellington Marathon Group Runs/Course Reccie
events.venue: 139A Oriental Pde, Oriental Bay, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Wellington Scottish and Sparkle Fitness are hosting a group long run and inviting all those who are doing any of the Gazley Volkswagen events along.
Join us for the first official course recce in the lead-up to the Wellington Marathon.
Starting from Freyberg, this guided run will cover a key section of the course and give you the opportunity to:
• Familiarise yourself with the terrain
• Understand how to approach pacing on this section
• Identify areas where effort needs to be controlled
• Ask training-related questions in an informal setting
This is not a race simulation. It’s a structured training run designed to build confidence and awareness.
All distances are welcome. The focus is on smart preparation and community support.

events.id: Opening the Dance Floor: Tutor training workshop-7 Colombo St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Opening the Dance Floor: Tutor training workshop
events.venue: 7 Colombo St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
This practical tutor training workshop explores how to create accessible, engaging dance environments for people of all abilities.
Together, we’ll explore a range of teaching approaches, including touch-based guidance, clear vocal direction, and effective visual cueing.
We’ll look at how to scaffold movement and learning for diverse abilities, and structure classes to support participation at every level.
This workshop will also highlight the value of support and volunteer tutors—particularly when working with larger groups—and how to integrate them effectively into your teaching practice.
Ideal for both new and experienced tutors, this session offers hands-on tools to help you open the dance floor to everyone.
WHEN: 10 am to 1 pm, Sunday 19th April
WHERE: Newtown Community Centre, Corner Columbo and Rintoul St, Newtown.
COST: $45
TO BOOK: please email sumara@widance.nz OR phone 021 050 1565

This show will be held in the Visitor Centre Foyer and is suitable for all ages, normal admission to the sanctuary applies. Overflow parking is available on Birdwood Street during the busy school holiday period.

events.id: Textured Bracelets - Taster Class-65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Textured Bracelets - Taster Class
events.venue: 65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
This lively, hands-on workshop is designed for complete beginners, but it’s also a great opportunity for more experienced students to enjoy a creative session making their own unique bangle!
Everyone will leave with a beautifully finished bracelet of their own preferred texture.
We’ll begin by experimenting on a sample strip of copper, where you’ll practice creating textures using hammers, punches and stamps. Once you’re confident you’ll move on to your main project: a 3mm wide flat strip bracelet in either sterling silver or copper, crafted entirely by you from start to finish. Along the way, you’ll learn essential jewellery making skills including measuring, texturing, soldering, shaping, and finishing.
Students are free to personalise their bangle with textures or stamped designs. This could be a simple hammered finish, decorative patterns, or meaningful words, names, dates, or poetry lines - making your bracelet truly one of a kind.
Beginners will work with the 3mm strip provided, ensuring a rewarding project that’s manageable to complete. Wider metal is much harder to form and solder, so we are learning to walk before we can run
Copper is included in the course fee, if you wish to work in silver you can purchase it on the day.
Experienced students may choose to challenge themselves by making a wider bracelet from flat strip or by creating additional bangles in silver wire. (Please note: extra silver for wider bangles is available to purchase at the workshop for experienced students only, and let us know if you wish to go wider so we can make sure we have the strip in stock).
This workshop is a taster class: perfect for anyone seeking a fun, creative experience with a beautiful result, or for those looking to dip their toes into jewellery making and build foundational skills.
Workshop fee $135, Early bird $122 till 22 March.

events.id: HAWTHORNE HEIGHTS | LONELY WORLD TOUR-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: HAWTHORNE HEIGHTS | LONELY WORLD TOUR
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
HAWTHORNE HEIGHTS  | LONELY WORLD TOUR
Celebrating the 20th Anniversary of If Only You Were Lonely — April 2026

Emo pioneers Hawthorne Heights are set to return to New Zealand in 2026 as part of their global Lonely World Tour, commemorating the 20th anniversary of their influential 2006 album If Only You Were Lonely. The band will perform the album in full, alongside a selection of fan favourites spanning their two-decade career.
Since its release, If Only You Were Lonely has become a defining record of the 2000s emo and post-hardcore movement, featuring anthems such as “Saying Sorry”, “Pens and Needles”, and “This Is Who We Are.” The anniversary tour offers fans the rare chance to experience the album live, in an intimate and high-energy setting.

Hawthorne Heights will play two New Zealand shows only:
Friday 17 April – Auckland | Double Whammy
Saturday 18 April – Wellington | San Fran

Tickets and VIP packages are available now through: waxstartouring.com & hawthorneheights.com

events.name: Yawner
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Wellington’s latest heavy-hitting R&B outfit, Yawner, bring tight, groove-driven energy and the striking vocal power of Eden Brown.

Formed in late 2024, the group has honed a sound that’s rooted in R&B, indie and jazz, and has now unveiled in their debut singles 'Monkey Say' and 'Subside' (releasing April 10th) ahead of their forthcoming 2026 EP. Focusing on a heavy-hitting but soothing craft, they’ve put together an enormous live set which is not to be missed.

Following up on their festival highlight performance at Cuba Dupa, they are treating fans to yet another special show, which will be their last with drummer Ryder Smith as he heads over to London.

They will be joined by the amazing Bri Louise and her enormously talented band.

Free entry, koha encouraged

events.id: Auditions: Noel Coward's Hay Fever--41.296180, 174.773379-Facebook
events.name: Auditions: Noel Coward's Hay Fever
events.long_description:
Hay Fever is a farcical comedy by legendary actor and writer Noel Coward. Set in the 1920s, the action takes place in an English manor house belonging to the Bliss family. Unbeknownst to each other, each member of the Bliss family has invited a guest to stay for the weekend. A series of misunderstandings leads to hilarious hijinks.
We are seeking a cast of 9 roles: 5 females and 4 males. The setting is 1920s England at a manor house in the country.

Directed by Susannah Donovan
Performance Dates July 1st – July 11th 2026 at The Gryphon Theatre.
Character Description:
Judith Bliss: Mother to Simon and Sorel, and wife of David Bliss. She has recently retired from acting on stage. Judith lives for drama. She is overly dramatic in everything she does. She invites Sandy Tyrell as a weekend guest. There is an opportunity for the actress playing Judith to perform a song.
Female, Age range 40+
David Bliss: Father of Sorel and Simon, and husband of Judith. He is a successful writer. Droll and self-absorbed. He invites Jackie Coryton as a weekend guest.
Male, Age range 40+
Sorel Bliss: Daughter of Judith and David. Wise beyond her years. She may be the only one who truly sees the Bliss family as they are, but that does not stop her from participating in the hijinks. She invites Richard Greatham as a weekend guest.
Female, Age range 20s.
Simon Bliss: Son of Judith and David. An artist who draws caricatures. Often sarcastic. He invites Myra Arundel as a weekend guest.
Male, Age range 20s.
Myra Arundel: Confident, sophisticated, and astute. She knows what she wants, but even she is not prepared for what the Bliss family throws at her. Invited for the weekend by Simon.
Female, Age range 30s.
Richard Greatham: An English diplomat. Reserved, and appears to be uptight. Invited for the weekend by Sorel.
Male, Age range 30’s – 40s.
Jackie Coryton: A young flapper. Sweet but not too sharp. Invited for the weekend by David.
Female, Age range 20s.
Sandy Tyrell: Athletic boxer with a crush on Judith. Good-natured and affable. Invited for the weekend by Judith.
Male, Age range 20’s – 30s.
Clara: Worked for most of her life as Judith’s dresser in the theatre. Now that Judith has retired she is now her housekeeper. Bad at her new job and quite rude to people.
Female, Age range 40s+
Please click here for further details and to book an audition time: https://www.wellingtonrepertory.org.nz/hay-fever-auditions/

Unbeknownst to each other, each member of the Bliss family has invited a guest to stay for the weekend.
Female, Age range 40+ David Bliss: Father of Sorel and Simon, and husband of Judith.
She may be the only one who truly sees the Bliss family as they are, but that does not stop her from participating in the hijinks.
Invited for the weekend by David.

events.id: Gary Wilby; Reflections, Miniatures and Soundscapes-62 Friend St, Karori, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Gary Wilby; Reflections, Miniatures and Soundscapes
events.venue: 62 Friend St, Karori, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description: Gary Wilby has recently retired after nearly 50 years in the classroom. He taught Music both in the classroom and as an Itinerant teacher – mostly in secondary schools. He was fortunate in 1995 to be appointed to the Composer in Schools position which resulted in many works for students to play. Gary graduated from the University of Canterbury with a MA(Hons). He plays piano and cello and while he has in the past sung in choirs, he now enjoys the annual NZCF Wellington May Workshop. Gary has played in many ensembles and has been in the Wellington City Orchestra (formerly Wellington Chamber Orchestra) since 1986. He formerly was for 15 years the Music Director and Conductor of the Capital Performing Arts Orchestra. He has regularly played with “Opus 3 Trio” for the past decade. This will be Gary's third performance in Futuna Chapel.

events.name: Working Bee - Birdwood Reserve - Sunday 19 April 2026
events.long_description:
Time for another working bee! We'll be back at Birdwood Reserve, opposite the Zealandia Overflow carpark.
This is an important site for Karori Kaitiaki, as it's the home of the future Birdwood Reserve track. Volunteers can either climb down the gully to help clear weeds, or can comfortably work on the street-level planted areas around the carpark.

events.id: Songwriters' Open Mic at Moon-167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Songwriters' Open Mic at Moon
events.venue: 167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Songwriters’ Open Mic at Moon
The first of the 2026 series of Songwriters’ Open Mic sessions at Moon will be on Sunday April 19 kicking off shortly after the bar opens at 4pm. There will be sessions each month through until November, on the afternoons preceding the Songwriters' Showcase concerts scheduled for the evenings.
These sessions are solely for folk to perform their own original songs. So what’s the drill? Turn up, make yourself known to whoever appears to be in charge, and get your name on the list. Performances will then be in the order called by the organisers - no pre-booking of times, or reserving spots. Sets will be either 2 or 3 songs depending on the level of demand.
So regardless whether you are a “newby” taking your first steps beyond your bedroom, or an “old hand” wanting to test drive new material, or just get some “open wicket practice”, this is the open mic for you. If you fancy getting to play one of the Showcase concerts this is the right place to audition. And it's not just a chance to perform but to meet and get to know other songwriters around town.
Performers at the open mic get entry to the following Songwriters show for $10 (discounted from the regular punters price of $15.
The Poneke Songwriters Collective thanks Moon Bar for supporting these events and continuing to support the local music community
As the cool kids say “be there or be quadrilateral”.
Pic - Random “guy with a guitar”

events.id: Saosin | WEL | Celebrating 20 Years & The Hits | with Senses Fail | San Fran-Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Saosin | WEL | Celebrating 20 Years & The Hits | with Senses Fail | San Fran
events.venue: Edilsons Building, 171 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Orange County post-hardcore trailblazers Saosin will return to Australia in April 2026 for a national headline tour, celebrating 20 years of their iconic self-titled album - playing the album in full as well as the hits. Joining them across all dates is special guests and post-hardcore legends Senses Fail. Tickets on sale Thursday 26th February 8am local time via Goodvibes Promotions
Saosin quickly emerged as a powerful voice in the post-hardcore and alternative rock scenes, gaining immediate underground acclaim from the get-go. Following a shift in vocalists, Cove Reber joined the band and fronted their breakout self-titled album in 2005, which featured genre-defining tracks like "Voices" and "You’re Not Alone". The album's success propelled Saosin into mainstream recognition, earning them a dedicated global fanbase and cementing their status as one of the most influential bands of their era.
Saosin were first noticed by New Zealand fans at the Taste of Chaos tour at St James in 2006 alongside acts Taking Back Sunday, Underoath, Anti-Flag, Thursday and Senses Fail. They continued to come back to these shores in 2010 with Bless the Fall for one Auckland show
Now, 20 years on they’ll perform their self-titled album in full across New Zealand alongside their biggest hits next April.
Since reuniting with vocalist Cove Reber in 2024, Saosin has reignited the fervent energy that defined their most influential years. With Reber back at the helm, Saosin is redefining their future. The chemistry among the original lineup has sparked creative momentum, leading to new material, revitalized performances, and expanded vision. The band is once again a driving force in post-hardcore, proving that while time may pass, authenticity and connection endure. This chapter in Saosin’s journey is both a homecoming and a launching point for what’s next.

Supporting Saosin on this special run is Senses Fail, These guys are also no strangers to New Zealand having been here for the Taste of Chaos tour in 2006.. The band have been called any number of things across their near 25 years as a band – emo, screamo, post-hardcore, metalcore, pop punk. It’s testament to the band’s perpetual evolution as much as it is their steely endurance – a quality which frontman Buddy Nielsen has required bottomless reserves of along the ride. It’s through the songs of Senses Fail that Nielsen has forever made sense of and peace with life’s highs and lows; a journey that began in the leafy suburbs of Ridgewood, New Jersey with the iconic Gold-selling debut album Let It Enfold You, and which has since seen Senses Fail become one of rock music’s most cherished, celebrated names.

Bringing together two giants of their genre, don't miss Saosin and Senses Fail do what they do best - tickets available Thursday 26th February 2026 at 8am local time via Goodvibes Promotions
Tickets on sale.
Saosin 2026 Tour Dates
Sat 18th Apr - Studio The Venue (Auckland)
Sun 19th April - San Fran (Wellington)
Mon 20th April Sydenham Underpass (Christchurch)

events.id: Jewellery-making on Monday Nights - 6 weeks block, Level 2+-65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Jewellery-making on Monday Nights - 6 weeks block, Level 2+
events.venue: 65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Our popular weekend block classes are the best way for keen makers to get into jewellery making in a lively, warm and friendly environment.
This class is for level 2+ and advanced students. It is not open to beginners.
Students must have completed at least two 4-week blocks or a 6-week fabrication block. If you are unsure of your level because you may have done a few different shorter classes, please contact us and we can advise you.
Students will be supported in further developing their jewellery-making skills in the areas that interest them.
Level 2 students learn to solder more competently on their own using chainmaking as a way to develop their fine soldering skills. They may create a simple stone setting or ring alongside this challenge project so they have something to hone their skills that they are familiar with, as well as taking on a new skill. Their project could be a pendant on a handmade chain, a charm bracelet or a more complex bezel setting - for example a teardrop or soft square stone to take on bezels with corners!
Level 3 and above students are encouraged to work on their design skills and try new and challenging techniques.
This class fee does NOT include materials for any student. All metals and stones as well as quality findings and chains are available for purchase each week in the studio. Students are encouraged to keep their silver scrap and find ways to use it thriftily in future projects!
Our booking system asks for payment to be made via credit card, Afterpay or PayPal on booking. If you do not have access to these payment methods or need to pay your course fee over time please email us to arrange this
Course fee $355, Early bird $325 till 22 March.

events.venue: 166 Main Rd, Tawa, Wellington 5028, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!
Join us for a creative evening of drinking
and painting
in the heart of Wellington! No previous painting experience necessary. Get your creative juices flowing and your brush strokes going!
Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.
Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.
What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!
We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.
For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to
[[https://paintvine.co.nz/]

events.id: Moonday Quiz hosted by Young Gho$t - fundraising for Kai Ika-167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Moonday Quiz hosted by Young Gho$t - fundraising for Kai Ika
events.venue: 167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Mondays don’t have to be a drag - come flex your brain at Moon with quizmaster Young Gho$t. Expect two music rounds, plus general knowledge, pop culture, geography and whatever else we feel like throwing at you.
It’s $2 to play, with every dollar going to a different charity each month. Teams are capped at 8, so round up your smartest mates and grab a table.
The quiz kicks off at 7pm, but come early for food - we’ve got pizza and drink deals to keep your neurons firing. Prizes include Moon show tickets and more!
Book your table at newtownmoon.co.nz
This month's quiz proceeds will be going to Kai Ika! You may recognise them from their Cans for Kai service, filleting, and free fish heads. The project utilises fish heads, frames and offal which were previously going to waste. Since first established in September 2016 over 600,000 kg of previously discarded fish parts have been collected from various sources and redistributed to needy families and community groups all over Auckland. Due to popularity, The Kai Ika Project has expanded to include a satellite operation in Wellington and the Hawkes Bay. Find out more about this incredible organisation here: https://kaiika.co.nz/who-are-we/

events.id: Evening meeting - A mycology of New Zealand in 10 fungi-101 Victoria St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Evening meeting - A mycology of New Zealand in 10 fungi
events.venue: 101 Victoria St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Speaker: Dr Geoff Ridley. Fungi are often overlooked. Most people only become aware of them when there is a problem, the bathroom ceiling goes mouldy, the roses need spraying because of the black spot, mushrooms sprout all over the lawn. The bigger role that fungi play in our lives is missed. This is the story of ten little-known fungi that are important biologically, culturally, historically, and economically in Aotearoa.
Geoff is a mycologist who studied the larger fungi in the Ōrongorongo Valley for his PhD at Victoria University of Wellington, and as a forest mycologist and pathologist at the Forest Research Institute in Rotorua. He currently works in the nationally significant collections of fungi held by Manaaki Whenua – Landcare Research. His personal interest is in discovering the fungi of the Kaiwharawhara Valley in Wellington.

events.id: Wilkie Mac and Penni Feather at Acoustic Routes-1 Norman Ln, Johnsonville, Wellington 6037, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Wilkie Mac and Penni Feather at Acoustic Routes
events.venue: 1 Norman Ln, Johnsonville, Wellington 6037, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Monday 20 April, Johnsonville Club, 1 Norman Lane (off Johnsonville Road), Johnsonville, Wellington, 7:30 pm. Parking up the drive at the venue, bus and train nearby.
Entry: $20/$15 for members. Cash only (no eftpos facilities).
Raglan musicians WilkieMac and Penni Feather have been touring as a double bill and bring their combined show to Acoustic Routes, performing separately and together.
Penni Feather
From English trad and blues, to her originals in a contemporary folk style, Penni loves to connect with an audience through song. Known for her interpretive skills, she brings a wide vocal range and emotional depth to her performances.
After starting out in folk music in Hamilton and Auckland, in the 2010s Penni was again active on the North Island folk circuit and guested at Wellington, Aongatete and Hamilton folk festivals. More recently Penni has had a one-woman cabaret show in the Hamilton Arts Festival and performed solo in the inaugural Raglan Blues Festival.
WilkieMac
WilkieMac are Lynne Wilkins and Michael Mackinnon. The duo has a deserved reputation in NZ’s folk scene for their arrangements, with their original songs and Celtic roots to the fore. Recent highlights include being the featured concert guest at the 2025 Te Rangi Festival in Hawkes Bay and opening for Australian band Ukulele Death Squad for one of their pre-WOMAD tour gigs.
Michael provides the solid backdrop on guitar, while Lynne brings a varied soundscape with harmonica, flutes, whistles, guilele (a six-string ukulele) and guitar. Topped with their blended vocals, a WilkieMac performance is bound to leave you wanting more.
Penni Feather and WilkieMac collaborated for sets at the 2025 Hamilton Arts Festival. They enjoyed weaving instruments and harmonies on each other’s songs so much, they teamed up again for gigs that have since been taking them around the North Island.
Note: The concert will be on the first floor, with access via stairs or the lift. (We are told the lift is back in action!) You may have to press the button at the side of the door to gain access to the building.

Supper is not provided, but you’re welcome to purchase drinks, tea and coffee and snacks at the bar. If you want to purchase alcohol, a member of the Johnsonville Club will need to sign you in. Feel free to make your purchases on the way in and bring them up to the room.

events.venue: Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Register and come along to this workshop led by Dr Fahimeh McGregor, and learn how AI supports everyday office tasks.
Registrations required. Full room details on registration. You will need to bring your own laptop. REGISTER HERE: https://tinyurl.com/mr2dzpjc
About the presenter:
Dr Fahimeh McGregor is the founder of DELTA Informed Decisions and a lecturer at Massey University Business School. With more than 20 years of experience across the public and private sectors, she specialises in AI readiness, data strategy, and helping organisations translate emerging technologies into practical and productive outcomes.
Fahimeh works with a wide range of organisations — from large institutions to independent business owners — helping them cut through complexity and integrate AI and digital tools into their operations in ways that are purposeful, time-saving, and responsible. Her focus is on building genuine capability rather than chasing technology for its own sake.
She is passionate about making AI accessible and practical for people willing to engage with it thoughtfully, and brings a practical, human-centred perspective to every workshop, consulting engagement, and classroom.

events.id: Brazilian Zouk - Improvers - Variations Yoyo and Bonus-60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Brazilian Zouk - Improvers - Variations Yoyo and Bonus
events.venue: 60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Come and join us for Improvers Zouk Block C (5 weeks).
We have some great new content for you this year and will be building on foundational movements.
The complete Improvers course consists of 6 different Blocks (A-F).
Each block focuses on a different aspect and technique of Brazilian Zouk and so they can be completed independently of each other.
Our recommendation is to complete all 6 Blocks before progressing onto the Brazilian Zouk Deep Dive classes.
In Improvers Block B we will introduce you to cool variations on the Yoyo and Bonus Movements, as well as some exercises on responding to extension, compression.
===Prices and payment:
- Prepaid 5 week Block cost is $90 waged or $75 for unwaged/students
- Ad hoc cost per class is $20 waged or $18 unwaged/students
Online payments can be made to Adam Polaczuk
Acct: 03-0531-0559348-25
Details: Your name and ZoukImpC
===Location:
Classes are in Footnote dance studio, 60 Dixon Street
===Housekeeping:
- You should have completed the 4 beginner Class blocks before joining Improvers classes
- Drop in or new students, please get in contact before the start of class to let us know you are interested so we can assess your level and organise a private class if necessary to catch you up. If you just show up on the night, you are welcome to watch the class but will not be able to join in (this is to respect the learning of our regular students as the classes are progressive)

events.id: Flight of the Conchords Wellington | Night 3-92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Flight of the Conchords Wellington | Night 3
events.venue: 92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The New Zealand warm-up shows precede Bret and Jemaine’s three Southern California appearances in May: Thursday 7th May at the Santa Barbara Bowl, and their two Sold-Out appearances at the 2026 edition of the Netflix Is A Joke Festival at The Greek Theatre in Los Angeles on Saturday 9th May, and Sunday 10th May. These are the Flight of the Conchords' first performances in 10 years.
New Zealanders Bret McKenzie and Jemaine Clement debuted on HBO in 2005 in an edition of the comedy series “One-Night Stand,” returning to the network in 2007 for the debut season of their acclaimed, Emmy-nominated series “Flight of the Conchords,” which followed fictionalized versions of their lives in New York City.
The duo also signed a worldwide deal with Sub Pop Records for their recorded music, going on to release the Grammy-winning EP, The Distant Future in 2007, followed by two full-lengths and a live album - their self-titled debut in 2008, I Told You I Was Freaky in 2009, and Live In London in 2019. They also headlined in the US and the UK multiple times, including their wildly successful “Flight of the Conchords Sing Flight of the Conchords Tour” in 2016.
The Conchords’ have also enjoyed individual success. Clement is the co-creator (and occasional guest star) of the comedy horror TV shows Wellington Paranormal and What We Do In The Shadows. He had a recurring role in FX’s Legion, and appeared in films like Men In Black 3, The Minecraft Movie, Megan 2.0, and most recently as “Dr. Garvin” in the last two Avatar films (The Way of Water in 2022; and Fire and Ash in 2025).
McKenzie was the songwriter for The Muppets, which won him an Academy Award® for Best Original Song for “Man or Muppet,” and “Muppets Most Wanted.” He's also written songs for TV and film, including The Simpsons and The Minecraft Movie. Bret also recently released two solo albums for Sub Pop - Songs Without Jokes in 2022 and Freak Out City in 2024, and has been touring live in New Zealand, Australia, the United States, and the United Kingdom.

events.id: Jason Leong: In Laughing Memory-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Jason Leong: In Laughing Memory
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
DOORS OPEN 6:30pm
SHOW START 7:00pm

After losing one of his best friends to cancer, Dr Jason Leong did a very Dr Jason Leong thing to process his grief: write a comedy show about it. 
Join him in his most personal hour yet, as he navigates loss and love with laughter. 
Dr Jason Leong is a medical doctor turned comedian and he never fails to bring that up, unprompted. He has had two Netflix specials, performed at Just For Laughs Montreal in 2022, and won the 7th Annual International Hong Kong Comedy Competition. He tours around the world because he loves comedy and ultimately, money.

events.id: Karli Tyla - Free Live Music Wednesdays-167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Karli Tyla - Free Live Music Wednesdays
events.venue: 167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Lift your midweek with Moon's Free Live Music Wednesdays, your weekly escape into the sonic stratosphere!
Every week, we feature a rotating lineup of incredible musicians - this week we have Karli Tyla!
Karli is an indie-pop singer-song writer from Ngāti Whātua ki Ōrākei. Karli recently opened at Party4t, a fundraising event for PATHA. When she is not maintaining a below average gpa as a law student she is spending as much time as possible in te taiao. Karli writes about her experiences as a takatāpui wahine Māori; ranging from situationship heartbreaks to studying at the heart of empire and everything in between.
Come by early for our $15 margherita pizza specials, served hot 4-9pm.
Perfect for date night, catch-ups, or just floating solo through the sound waves. Wednesdays at Moon are the place to orbit!

events.id: Stargazing 201-40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Stargazing 201
events.venue: 40 Salamanca Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Have you ever wanted to look a little deeper into the night sky, not just to identify what you’re seeing, but to understand how it all works? In this brand new two‑hour live planetarium session, we’ll explore the stories, science, and structures that shape our view of the cosmos.
Stargazing 201 builds on the foundations of our beginner programme, taking you further into seasonal constellations, cultural astronomy, the lives and evolution of stars, and the possibility of life beyond Earth. You’ll also journey outward through the observable universe and discover how telescopes allow us to see far beyond the limits of our eyes.
Sit back and enjoy an immersive guided tour of the universe under our planetarium dome, brought to life with our high‑definition laser projectors. Designed for curious minds ready to go further, Stargazing 201 is ideal for those who’ve attended Stargazing 101 but remains accessible and engaging for all.
Includes:
- Intermission with refreshments
- Access to Space Place’s galleries and interactive exhibits
20% discount at Space Place Shop (excludes food and already discounted items)
Doors open 6:30pm | Event 7:00pm-9:00pm | Space Place | R16

events.id: Ockham Awards Author Panel: Ingrid Horrocks and Khadro Mohamed-Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Ockham Awards Author Panel: Ingrid Horrocks and Khadro Mohamed
events.venue: Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Ockham New Zealand Book Awards celebrate and highlight some of our country’s brightest literary talents. On the 23rd of April we will be hosting a very special event with two of the longlisted authors, Ingrid Horrocks and Khadro Mohamed.
This panel will be authors asking authors the big, deep important questions about their work, practice and creative life, providing a unique, intimate, and in-depth insight into the creative processes behind their award nominated works.
Where: Ngā Pou Ruahine room, Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Central Library
This is a free event and all are welcome. We expect this event to be popular and seating is limited; please arrive early to ensure a seat.
Meet our authors:
Ingrid Horrocks:
Ingrid's many books include the memoir 'Where We Swim', 'Women Wanderers and the Writing of Mobility', as well as two collections of poetry. Amongst her many accolades and accomplishments, she has a PhD from Princeton University, and taught at Massey University for over a decade, where she was made a Professor in Creative Writing and English. Her debut collection of short stories 'All Her Lives' is shortlisted for this year's Ockham's fiction award.
Khadro Mohamed:
Khadro burst onto the literary scene with her exceptional collection of poetry 'We’re All Made of Lightning', which quickly gained rave reviews and went on to win the Jessie Mackay Prize for the best first book of poetry at the 2023 Ockhams. Her debut novel 'Before the Winter Ends' was longlisted for this year’s Ockham’s Jann Medlicott Acorn Prize for Fiction.

We expect this event to be popular and seating is limited; please arrive early to ensure a seat.

events.name: Sleaford Mods tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Handsome Tours
Handsome Tours are proud to present Sleaford Mods 2026 Australian & New Zealand Tour
This will be Sleaford Mods first time back to Australian and New Zealand soil since May and June of 2023, where they performed at events like Sydney's Vivid LIVE and Hobart's Dark Mofo.
“We took a year off ’cause I needed to get my nails did, and now we’re itching to get back out there and play live”, says Sleaford Mods' Jason Williamson. “We released a new single Megaton last month, it’s an absolute banger so we’re buzzing to play that live for the first time. Who knows, we might have a few more new tracks to drop by then. Either way, it’s been a while since we toured, and we’re gagging for it.”
Sleaford Mods has partnered with PLUS1 to support War Child. $1.15 inc GST from every ticket sold will help War Child to protect, educate, and stand up for the rights of children affected by war.
www.warchild.org.uk

events.id: A Place to Sit – Opening-26 Constable St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: A Place to Sit – Opening
events.venue: 26 Constable St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
As days pass us by and the seasons change, it is a gift to be able to crop into the details of our daily landscapes. A kitchen table surrounded by family, or the bush as we traverse up into its hills - there is tangibility of time and much needed stillness to be found in both Aida Smith and Benjamin Cotter Dephoff’s canvases. Twentysix Gallery is pleased to present their latest works in ‘A Place to Sit’ - the two artists' depictions of small, everyday rituals grounding us - providing a texture and meaning in what might seem like ordinary moments.
The two oil painters work with introspection and the rhythm of slow living, documenting the subtle shifts across our day to day. While Ben’s paintings are attuned to how time and weather shape his landscapes, Aida’s scenes from home seem suspended in time, with an ever so subtle mirroring of the changes in light, warmth and routine that mark our passage across the changing seasons. A tonal warmth spreading across them both as we enter autumn - reflective, inward and a time for slowing down.
Ben bases his practice in the bush, an ‘on the ground’ report of Aotearoa’s wild trails and the steps taken on them. A deep breath looking down from the top of a summit, as well as floral close ups from on the way up. His works are deep in tone, with the dark greens and brown of the landscape and wood walls in the firelight of a DOC hut, as a base for each scene he depicts in his perfectly detailed, almost photorealistic style.
Aida’s lens is closer up. The scene while sitting down with a cup of tea and a loved one or a candle lit in the evening - her people seem to have just left the frame, their presence felt at the edges. A looser stroke, a wider shadow - warming light that emanates from her canvases. Both Ben and Aida’s works are underscored with a recognition of how important it is to recharge - to leave the weekday rush for a while and take time in solitude, or else surrounded by your community, whether it is in the wild winds of the outdoors, or warm inside - intentional stillness, reflection and appreciation is nourishment for an active mind.
The exhibition runs from 24 April – 16 May 2026, with an opening celebration on Thursday, 23 April at 5pm.

A kitchen table surrounded by family, or the bush as we traverse up into its hills - there is tangibility of time and much needed stillness to be found in both Aida Smith and Benjamin Cotter Dephoff’s canvases.
Twentysix Gallery is pleased to present their latest works in ‘A Place to Sit’ - the two artists' depictions of small, everyday rituals grounding us - providing a texture and meaning in what might seem like ordinary moments.
The two oil painters work with introspection and the rhythm of slow living, documenting the subtle shifts across our day to day.
While Ben’s paintings are attuned to how time and weather shape his landscapes, Aida’s scenes from home seem suspended in time, with an ever so subtle mirroring of the changes in light, warmth and routine that mark our passage across the changing seasons.
Ben bases his practice in the bush, an ‘on the ground’ report of Aotearoa’s wild trails and the steps taken on them.

events.id: Double Book Launch: Dinah Hawken and James Brown-57 Willis St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Double Book Launch: Dinah Hawken and James Brown
events.venue: 57 Willis St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us to launch two incisive new poetry collections - Peace & Quiet by Dinah Hawken and New Days for Old by James Brown.
New Days for Old is a life story in small scenes. Funny, moving, erotic, political and undeniably odd, it turns our most familiar-feeling days into something altogether new.
Each scene in this book is like a 1-minute pop song: its depths are at first easy to miss. But as the story proceeds, the grand scheme of things hoves into view: we are born, we crawl and then are carried away, and everything is a-shimmer, even the disappointments.
Dinah Hawken's Peace & Quiet queries the use of poetry in times of ecological and political turbulence, invoking both human voices and the voices — ‘the silt and the slash’ — of the natural world.
Powerful and illuminating, these poems show that peace, gentleness and reflection are a form of resistance.

events.name: Sleaford Mods with Dropper | Meow Nui, Wellington - SOLD OUT!
events.venue: 92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Handsome Tours are pleased to present Sleaford Mods on their long awaited return to Australian shores.
This will be Sleaford Mods first time back to Australian and New Zealand soil since May and June of 2023, where they performed at events like Sydney's Vivid LIVE and Hobart's Dark Mofo.
“We took a year off ’cause I needed to get my nails did, and now we’re itching to get back out there and play live”, says Sleaford Mods' Jason Williamson. “We released a new single Megaton last month, it’s an absolute banger so we’re buzzing to play that live for the first time. Who knows, we might have a few more new tracks to drop by then. Either way, it’s been a while since we toured, and we’re gagging for it.”
'Megaton', the band’s first new material since the release of 2023 album UK GRIM, is available to stream now. Proceeds from the song are going to War Child, a commitment the band have deepened with this tour by partnering with PLUS1 to give $1 from every ticket sold to the charity.

events.id: City Nature Challenge 2026-15 Mercer St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: City Nature Challenge 2026
events.venue: 15 Mercer St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Wellington is joining the world in the global wildlife count! Will you join us? All you need to do is
explore,
snap, and
share.
A bunch of local events scattered throughout Te Upoko o te Ika (Wellington Region) will help you do just that! Throughout 24-27 April, we are teaming up with other nature enthusiasts and experts to bring local City Nature Challenge-inspired events to you. Updates on community events near you (and some spot prizes!!!) will be posted to this event page.
Annually, the City Nature Challenge invites cities from across the world to compete to see who can log the most nature observations on iNaturalist over four days.
Not only is this challenge a fun way to explore nature in our own backyard, but knowing what species are in our city and where they are helps us study and protect them.
Taking part is simple:
explore wildlife Friday 24 to Monday 27 April
take a photo of what you find
share it to iNaturalist app or website
For more information on the City Nature Challenge, visit our website

A tonal warmth spreading across them both as we enter autumn - reflective, inward and a time for slowing down.

A deep breath looking down from the top of a summit, as well as floral close ups from on the way up. His works are deep in tone, with the dark greens and brown of the landscape and wood walls in the firelight of a DOC hut, as a base for each scene he depicts in his perfectly detailed, almost photorealistic style.
Aida’s lens is closer up. The scene while sitting down with a cup of tea and a loved one or a candle lit in the evening - her people seem to have just left the frame, their presence felt at the edges. A looser stroke, a wider shadow - warming light that emanates from her canvases. Both Ben and Aida’s works are underscored with a recognition of how important it is to recharge - to leave the weekday rush for a while and take time in solitude, or else surrounded by your community, whether it is in the wild winds of the outdoors, or warm inside - intentional stillness, reflection and appreciation is nourishment for an active mind.

events.id: Rova tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Rova tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Cream Events
THIS IS THE HOO. ROVA is bringing it home.
After shutting down dancefloors across the UK and Europe, Aotearoa producer & DJ ROVA returns home for THIS IS THE HOO, a full scale NZ tour hitting Dunedin, Wellington, Auckland and Christchurch this April.
Since discovering his passion for music production in 2019, Rova has quickly become one of the fastest growing names in drum and bass. Each release showcases his distinct flair for pushing bass music boundaries, with his sound now turning heads around the globe.
With every show, he creates an electrifying atmosphere, blending new sounds with his signature style. His ability to connect with fans through his hard hitting live sets has led to sell out shows across Europe, Australia and New Zealand, this tour marks a full circle moment.
THIS IS THE HOO isn’t just a tour. It’s a homecoming, a statement, and a reminder that Rova is firmly in his moment.

events.id: Conservation Kōrero: Dark Skies-29 Waiapu Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Conservation Kōrero: Dark Skies
events.venue: 29 Waiapu Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Zealandia Te Māra a Tāne and the Wellington Astronomical Society have teamed up to bring you an evening of ‘Dark Skies’.
Astronomer Holly McClelland will begin the evening with a talk on the importance of dark skies, both to the natural ecosystem and to human health.
Illustrated with stunning image from the Cretney Observatory near Martinborough, Holly will introduce us to the night sky, so the next time you are looking to the stars you can not only find the Southern Cross, but also the Galactic Kiwi.
Weather permitting, we will then have an hour of telescope viewing from the Zealandia carpark. Alternatively, if the weather isn’t ideal, you’ll get the chance to get an up-close look at some of the incredible astronomical equipment used by the Wellington Astronomical Society. WAS members will be available to answer your questions.
About Holly:
Holly is an astronomer and science communicator. She has a Bachelor of Science in Astronomy from the University of Canterbury, was a senior astronomy guide in Tekapo, leading groups on once-in-a-lifetime stargazing experiences. Holly is also on the council of the Wellington Astronomy Society, helping to make events like this possible.
Please note: If the weather is clear for star gazing, the Visitor Centre carpark will be closed off for this activity. Parking is still available at our overflow carparking on Birdwood Street, but we encourage guests to consider public transport, taxi and rideshare options.
Doors open at 5:30pm with complimentary mocktails, talk begins at 6pm.

events.id: Tom Callwood | Ant Donaldson | Lucien Johnson | Jonny Marks | Dayle Jellyman-272 Taranaki St, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Tom Callwood | Ant Donaldson | Lucien Johnson | Jonny Marks | Dayle Jellyman
events.venue: 272 Taranaki St, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Five of Pōneke's finest improvisors converge on the Pyramid Club to channel "electronic space jazz"!
-
Tom Callwood (double bass) is an improvisor and collaborator who has performed throughout NZ and the UK. He’s a regular in the local experimental and popular music scenes with bands including The Phoenix Foundation, Teeth & Devils Gate Outfit.
-
Anthony Donaldson is one of this country's great drummers and a pioneer of improvised music in Aotearoa. He has been a creative force in Wellington music for over four decades and continues to push the boundaries of accepted musical norms.
-
Lucien Johnson is a New Zealand saxophonist, composer, and theatre maker. In recent years he has focussed on creating his own unique jazz sound, shrouded in wonder and mystique. His last album, “Wax///Wane” received rave reviews internationally, with France Musique calling it “Bewitching and Astonishing” and London Jazz News describing it as “Mystical, a touch retro and increasingly spellbinding”.
-
Dayle Jellyman is a keyboardist, guitarist, composer and man of many bands - from regular jazz sessions to the positive punk of Love Party and the apocalyptic metal of Fvkvshima.
-
Vocalist and electronic musician Jonny Marks has been a key figure in the creative music scene in Wellington since the early 2000s. Modulating his vocal chords like a synthesizer, Jonny moves between sub-bass drones to ring modulated whistle tones with his voice alone - and now in a spellbinding combination with a beloved Synthi AKS.
events.description:
Five of Pōneke's finest improvisors converge on the Pyramid Club to channel "electronic space jazz"!
- Tom Callwood (double bass) is an improvisor and collaborator who has performed throughout NZ and the UK.
He’s a regular in the local experimental and popular music scenes with bands including The Phoenix Foundation, Teeth & Devils Gate Outfit.
- Anthony Donaldson is one of this country's great drummers and a pioneer of improvised music in Aotearoa.
He has been a creative force in Wellington music for over four decades and continues to push the boundaries of accepted musical norms.

events.id: ANZAC Day - 2026 Open Day-66 Wrights Hill Rd, Karori, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: ANZAC Day - 2026 Open Day
events.venue: 66 Wrights Hill Rd, Karori, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
NEWS RELEASE :
FOR IMMEDIATE USE - April 2026
ANZAC DAY - OPEN DAY- SATURDAY 25th April: 10AM - 4PM.
Wellington’s historic Wrights Hill Fortress in Karori is open for public inspection again on ANZAC Day, SATURDAY, April 25th. The World War Two coastal battery first opened to the public in 1989 and will be accessible between 10am - 4pm.
On ANZAC Day people are invited to self-guide themselves through the tunnels and gun emplacements. The walk usually takes about one hour. Bring a torch with you for some fun!
Visitors will be able to explore the 620 metres of underground tunnels with a free map and history pamphlet. They will see the engine room, shell stores, plotting rooms and the huge gun pit where Gun Number 1 used to stand, as well as a replica of the 10-metre-long gun barrel.
Also on show will be historic photos, and a DVD of how a 9.2-inch gun operates will be showing as well. The World War Two Fortress will also have period themed displays. It’s only open to the public on four Open Days a year.
Wrights Hill Fortress was built from 1942 to protect Wellington from Japanese bombardment. In the early 1960’s, the guns were cut up for scrap and sold, ironically, to the Japanese.
Admission is $20 for a family of two adults and three children under 15, or $10 per adult and $5 per child. (Sorry no EFTPOS -CASH ONLY) Access is via Karori Road, Campbell Street, and Wrights Hill Road. All proceeds go to the continuing restoration of the Fortress. There is limited car parking at the summit of Wrights Hill. Please Park sensibly and use walking tracks.
Visitors to the Fortress in Wrights Hill Reserve can also enjoy picnic sites and panoramic views from the top of the hill. There will be a BBQ sausage sizzle and soft drinks on sale. For safety reasons no jandals please.

events.name: The King of Pop Show: Michael Jackson Live Concert Experience
events.venue: The Opera House, 109 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Get ready for the ultimate thrill! Experience Michael Jackson like never before with TJ Cappola's electrifying live concert: Beyond Imagination! Feel the magic of the King of Pop as if he was performing right in front of you.
If you never had the chance to witness Michael Jackson live, “The King of Pop Show – Michael Jackson Live Concert Experience” offers the closest experience to reliving the legendary artist’s magic. This dynamic show continues to captivate audiences across Australia and Asia with its sold-out performances.
This spectacular and powerful show captures all the excitement of Michael performing live in concert. From the electrifying energy to the iconic dance moves and unforgettable hits like “Billie Jean,” “Thriller,” “Beat It,” and more, audiences are transported to the peak of MJ’s career.
TJ Cappola’s remarkable portrayal, vocals and persona, supported by a live band, professional dancers, replica costumes and a state-of-the-art light show and visuals that will leave you in awe! This experience is a true homage to the King of Pop that fans won’t want to miss. It’s a thrilling opportunity to immerse oneself in the music and spectacle that defined Michael Jackson’s unparalleled legacy.

events.id: Zwanze Day 2026 - Garage Project's Wild Workshop-1 Furness Ln, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Zwanze Day 2026 - Garage Project's Wild Workshop
events.venue: 1 Furness Ln, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
GP is thrilled to be the exclusive host of Zwanze Day for New Zealand in 2026!
There are two ways you can join Zwanze Day 2026 at the Wild Workshop:
GET TICKETS to a 90-minute guided tasting, at 1pm, hosted by Pete Gillespie and Dave Bell (Wild Workshop Head Brewer).
OR / and
Come by the Wild Workshop from 2.30pm for FREESTYLE TASTING.
– – – – – – – – – – –
We’ll be tapping Cantillon’s Zwanze beers at the Wild Workshop in Wellington on 25 April, alongside a primo selection of beers from the GP cellar - an event this prestigious calls for the best, and we’re pulling out all the stops.
Zwanze Day happens but once every two years. It’s a global beer event run by renowned Belgian brewery Cantillon; they make a unique, one-off beer especially for the event, and it is tapped simultaneously at select venues across almost two-dozen countries. The Wild Workshop is Cantillon's exclusive partner in New Zealand.
– – – – – – – – – – –
Join Zwanze Day 2026:
Get tickets to a guided tasting, hosted by Pete Gillespie and Dave Bell (Wild Workshop Head Brewer).
This session will run for 90 minutes from 1pm. Included in the ticket is a guided tasting of 9 beers (the two 2026 Zwanze beers, a few other Cantillon treats and some of GP’s best from the cellar), food to match and a tour of the Wild Workshop brewery. There are limited spots - get in quick.
Tickets linked above
OR / and
Come by the Wild Workshop from 2.30pm for freestyle tasting.
Free entry, open for spontaneous walk-ins (get it). Pay-as-you-go to work your way through the line-up, or get a Zwanze flight, with some delish food to boot.
– – – – – – – – – – – –
This is the event for lovers of craft beer at its finest.

events.id: Middle Age Spread-1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Middle Age Spread
events.venue: 1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
We start our celebrations with ‘Middle Age Spread’ by Sir Roger Hall directed by Susan Wilson. The 25th April has been purposely selected because Circa’s official opening was the 23rd April 1976 which was picked as it is the same as Shakespeare’s birthday. We will be celebrating after the play reading with birthday cakes and drinks, as part of the performance.
First performed at the original Circa Theatre in Harris Street in 1977, directed by Michael Haigh, starring Dorothy McKegg, Grant Tilly, Jane Waddell, Ray Henwood, Donna Akersten and John Reid. Three couples attend a dinner none of them wants to be at.
Middle Age Spread ran for 15 months in London’s West End, winning the Lawrence Olivier Award for Comedy of the Year. In 1978 it became a successful NZ film directed by John Reid. This brilliant and poignant tragicomedy still remains a very significantly relevant social commentary on New Zealand Society . . . what’s changed in Aotearoa in 50 years, and then again… What hasn’t?

events.id: NZUTSRL Fundraising Dinner-100 Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: NZUTSRL Fundraising Dinner
events.venue: 100 Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Important Update regarding the NZUTSRL Fundraising Dinner
We’ve made the tough call to postpone our upcoming fundraising dinner. To be transparent, we haven't seen the level of support or ticket sales needed to make the event viable at this stage.
We want to ensure that everything we do truly serves our community, so we’re taking a step back to recalibrate.
We need your input!
We want to hear from you. What should NZUTSRL be focusing on? What kind of events or initiatives do you want to see? Please take two minutes to fill out our feedback form: https://docs.google.com/.../1FAIpQLSeh8iffQYG.../viewform...
Still keen for a catch-up?
The dinner might be on hold, but the spirit isn't! We’re still hosting a get-together before the Warriors game.
• Where: Backbenchers Pub
• When: This Saturday, from 4:00 PM
• Why: To talk footy, grab a drink, and catch up before we head to the game.
No formalities, just good vibes. We’d love to see you there!
Thanks for your ongoing patience and support. Up the Wahs!

events.id: Faultline Ultra--Wellington NZ
events.name: Faultline Ultra
events.long_description:
Calling all athletes on foot or bike. National and international competitors are invited to test their endurance at Faultline Ultra. For runners and mountain bikers, it’s a dream come true — the ultimate stamina challenge set against the wildly beautiful Wellington landscape. 
Race options
In April 2026, the hills and trails around Wellington offer six possible routes for athletes to push their limits. Choose from the following lengths depending on your ability and appetite.
6km – Magnitude 6.0
12km – The Tremor
25km – After Shock
53km Ultra – Shake and Bake
103km Ultra – The Worlds End
100 Miler – Insanity
The fault lines around Wellington have pushed and pulled the land to create soaring peaks, rugged ridgelines, and bush-clad hills. Athletes can choose trails that wind through Wellington City, Karori, Porirua, Belmont, the Hutt River Trail, and Kāpiti. Sign up and compete — you’re likely to get the best after-match stories you will ever tell.

events.name: Hidde Van Wee tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Lacuna
Lacuna presents Dutch selector Hidde Van Wee live at Meow Nui.
One of the most exciting emerging names out of Europe’s underground, Hidde Van Wee lands in Wellington fresh off his latest Eastenderz release ‘Shamans Vision’. With a sound rooted in minimal house and rolling techno, his sets are driven by deep grooves, crisp percussion, and an instinct for building energy across the dancefloor.
Whether in the booth or in the studio, Hidde delivers a sound that’s tight, hypnotic, and built for late nights - the kind that lock you in and don’t let go.
Supporting on the night is Lacuna resident Jack Ritchie, bringing his signature selection, alongside Auckland-based duo Cash - a high-energy pairing making serious waves with their productions and club-ready sound.
Expect a full night of forward-thinking underground music, dialled-in energy, and a packed dancefloor from start to finish.

events.id: King of Pop Show — Michael Jackson Live Concert Experience-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: King of Pop Show — Michael Jackson Live Concert Experience
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s a global phenomenon hailed as the most electrifying celebration of the King of Pop’s music. ‘The King of Pop Show — Michael Jackson Live Concert Experience’ makes its way to New Zealand — and Wellington — for the first time. If you never had the chance to see Michael Jackson live, this is the closest you’ll ever get. From the famous moonwalk to the spine-tingling vocals, every moment of this production channels the spirit of the King of Pop at his peak. Witness the power of hits like Billie Jean, Thriller, Beat It, Smooth Criminal, and plenty more. 
The star of the show is TJ Cappola. His stunning vocals and commanding stage presence make for a flawless embodiment of Michael Jackson. With sold-out performances across Australia and Asia, thousands have been mesmerised by his performance. Backing TJ is a live band and world-class dancers. The replica costumes and cutting-edge lighting and visual display make this more than just a show — it’s an experience.

events.id: One New Zealand Warriors v Dolphins ANZAC match-Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: One New Zealand Warriors v Dolphins ANZAC match
events.venue: Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Make your ANZAC long weekend a winner by cheering on the One New Zealand Warriors at Hnry Stadium. Aotearoa’s favourite NRL team is heading south to give fans outside of Auckland the opportunity to see them play. The Hnry Stadium event is only the fourth time the team has staged the prestigious ANZAC Day game in Aotearoa. It’s going to be a rare chance to see them in action. Australian team the Dolphins come from Redcliffe in Queensland.  They are sure to bring the energy needed to make this an epic battle. One New Zealand Warriors have had a sensational season and are currently in fourth place on the NRL Premiership leaderboard. 
From Hnry Stadium all the way along the waterfront, Wellington will be buzzing for the long weekend. Head on down for top-tier footy, food, and entertainment. It’s a great way to celebrate the spirit of the ANZACs as two countries united in history reignite their trans-Tasman rivalry. Be there when the capital goes Warrior mode.

events.id: Natural Textured Rings-65 Abel Smith St, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Natural Textured Rings
events.venue: 65 Abel Smith St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Make your own personalised ring - a gift for yourself or someone special!
Learn the basic technique first in copper or brass, and then craft your final piece in silver!
Over this taster class, you’ll be guided through the process of heating and texturing metal, cutting and shaping with traditional jewellery tools. Use feathers, leaves, or fabrics to create a unique texture for a beautiful ring!
Learn to measure your finger, cut metal to length and texture it without rolling mill, hammers and punches. Then use a jewellers torch, sandpaper and polishing, and your beautiful new ring will be ready to wear.
This 3-hour class is ideal for beginners, groups of friends and visitors to the city.
Included in the class fee are copper and brass for practising on, a sterling silver ring strip and all consumables such as sandpaper, solder and chemicals. Extra metals are available for purchase in the studio.
Kids aged 12 (or so) are welcome to attend the class, but any person under the age of 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.
Workshop fee is $165, Early bird pricing $148 (ends 30 March).

events.id: Mushrooms and other fungi - guided walk--41.267300, 174.759000-Facebook
events.name: Mushrooms and other fungi - guided walk
events.long_description:
A walk through the gardens and bush at Ōtari Wilton’s Bush to explore and discover the ever-changing array of mushrooms and fungi that are an essential part of its biodiversity.
Meet at Tāne Whakapiripiri, Ōtari Visitor Centre. It is easy walking and family groups are welcome. We ask that dogs stay at home for everyones comfort and a smooth tour experience.
This is part of the Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush Trust’s annual guided walks programme. This year, they are on the fourth Sunday of the month, March – November, to celebrate 100 years of Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush. Join the Trust for only $20 for an individual or $30 for a double/family for a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits. www.owbt.nz/memberships-legacies/
Ōtari 100: Celebrating 100 years of native plants, conservation, and community. 1926-2026.

events.name: Ol' King Coleslaw...
events.venue: 18 Garrett St, Wellington
events.long_description:
SUNDAY JAZZ @ THE ROGUE!
OL’ KING COLE brings you the hits and celebrates the music of the great Nat ‘King’ Cole and more! The group was originally inspired specifically with a focus on Nat’s early drum-less trio, which has now expanded for this special show.
Featuring Wellington based vocalist Wallace and prolific drummer Mike Jensen, the join the core trio of Louis Thompson-Munn (piano/vocals), Robert Henderson (double bass) & Lockie Bennett (guitar).
A great and lively performance is always a guarantee with the spirit of the old house band alive, before TVs in bars and when people would dance the night away!
Music from 5pm, FREE ENTRY + KOHA encouraged.

events.id: Girl Next Door: the next chapter tour - Wellington-167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Girl Next Door: the next chapter tour - Wellington
events.venue: 167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Jenni Smith is hitting the road this autumn for her “Girl Next Door: The Next Chapter” Tour - an intimate run of shows celebrating the release of her long-awaited debut album, The Girl Next Door (out March 27).
Fresh from nationwide chart success and years of diary-style storytelling, Jenni invites you into the next chapter of her world - one filled with red lipstick confidence, hopeless romantic daydreams, and the kind of songs that feel like pages torn straight from her journal. Expect heartfelt ballads, kitchen-dance anthems, and the stories behind the songs that built this era.
The tour kicks off in Auckland at Ponsonby Social Club on March 28th - a special album release party the night after the record drops - before heading to Christchurch’s Wunderbar on April 19th, and Wellington’s Moon on April 29th.
Joining Jenni for this run are three incredible artists:
Auckland - Blake Stainton
Christchurch - Steffany Beck
Wellington - Emma Kate
Whether you’ve been here since the first Taylor Swift cover or just found your way to The Girl Next Door, this tour is about celebrating the journey so far - and stepping into what comes next.
The next chapter starts here.

events.id: Rory Stuart Trio-Garibaldi Club, 118 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Rory Stuart Trio
events.venue: Garibaldi Club, 118 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Undercurrent presents the Rory Stuart Trio!
New York-based jazz guitarist and composer Rory Stuart is joined by two stalwarts of the Wellington jazz scene: Mark Lockett on drums and Elijah Mulheron on bass.
Rory Stuart is described by Jazz Times as “Perhaps THE most innovative straight-ahead jazz guitarist to emerge in years” and listed by Scott Yanow as one of the greatest living jazz guitarists. He has led critically acclaimed groups and his recordings have received 4 star reviews (Downbeat) and been listed in “10 Best of the Year” (Coda). He has also performed as a co-leader or sideperson with a truly diverse group of jazz masters, from Brother Jack McDuff and Bill Doggett to George Garzone, Billy Harper, Larry Coryell, and his former students Robert Glasper and Becca Stevens, to Steve Coleman’s early MBASE group with Geri Allen and Cassandra Wilson.
Sunday Apr 26
Doors 5pm
Music 5:30pm
$15
Tickets available online and on the door!

events.description:
The Undercurrent presents the Rory Stuart Trio!
New York-based jazz guitarist and composer Rory Stuart is joined by two stalwarts of the Wellington jazz scene: Mark Lockett on drums and Elijah Mulheron on bass.
Rory Stuart is described by Jazz Times as “Perhaps THE most innovative straight-ahead jazz guitarist to emerge in years” and listed by Scott Yanow as one of the greatest living jazz guitarists.
He has led critically acclaimed groups and his recordings have received 4 star reviews (Downbeat) and been listed in “10 Best of the Year” (Coda).
He has also performed as a co-leader or sideperson with a truly diverse group of jazz masters, from Brother Jack McDuff and Bill Doggett to George Garzone, Billy Harper, Larry Coryell, and his former students Robert Glasper and Becca Stevens, to Steve Coleman’s early MBASE group with Geri Allen and Cassandra Wilson.

events.id: Held in Motion - Somatic Movement Series-1 Left Bank, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Held in Motion - Somatic Movement Series
events.venue: 1 Left Bank, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Held in Motion
A 5-Week Evening Somatic Movement Series for Women
April 13 – May 11
(Monday evenings)
6pm - 7.15 pm

Held in Motion is a five-week somatic movement series for women who want to deepen their relationship with body, expression, and emotion …especially if you feel disconnected, overwhelm, or unsure how to stay in contact with what you’re feeling
At its core, somatic movement is movement guided by internal experience rather than external form.
It is movement led by sensation, impulse, breath, rhythm, and nervous system cues, rather than choreography, performance, or aesthetics.
This space is about listening.
~ Listening to impulse.
~ Listening to instinct.
~ Listening to sensation before it becomes story.
~ Listening to breath, to boundary, to rhythm.
~ Listening to the subtle yes.
~ Listening to the quiet no.
~ Listening to the places that want to soften.
~ Listening to the places that want to move.
Through free movement (without performance), somatic exploration, nervous system awareness, and guided reflection, we explore how to remain in relationship with ourselves while moving.
Each evening is intentionally paced and thoughtfully facilitated, weaving together:
~ Nervous system literacy, gently and accessibly held
~ Nervous system regulation practices integrated throughout
~ Guided somatic movement and free exploration
~ Music that fuels expression
~ Journaling and reflection
~ Moments of softness and moments of wildness
~ Joy. Depth. Integration.

Together we will explore how to:
~ Strengthen internal resourcing
~ Reconnect to sensation
~ Expand capacity for feeling without overwhelm
~ Access playful aliveness
~ Strengthen embodied choice and agency
~ Integrate emotional experience through the body
---
This is for you if…
~ You want to feel more at home in your body.
~ You sense there is more expression inside you than you currently allow.
~ You feel caught in thinking and long for deeper connection with your whole body.
~ You are curious about movement as a pathway to emotional integration.
~ You are ready to listen to your body in a new way.
~~~
REGISTER
Please fill out this form to register: https://forms.gle/vvGR4XzbynNBHEoa7
Spaces are limited to allow for a deeper, more intimate experience.
Cost: $30–$40 per session ($120–$160 for the 4-week series)
Choose the rate that feels right for you. Sliding scale pricing is to make this space more accessible while supporting the container we’re creating together.
***If finances are an issue for you to attend this space, please email me directly at freedomofsoma@gmail.com so we talk further

~~~

Four weeks.
Evenings devoted to the body.
If you are curious about using movement as a way back to yourself, without pressure, without needing to be a “dancer,” without needing to be anything other than human, this space may be for you.

See you soon!
Fiona x

~~
About me, your facilitator:
Hi, I am Fiona
I am an Integrative Somatic Facilitator™, Somatic Dance Facilitator, and Therapeutic Yoga Teacher devoted to helping people come home to their bodies. Drawing on somatics, nervous system literacy, and embodied movement, I hold spaces that support regulation, expression, and deeper self-connection.
Alongside my facilitation work, I collaborate with SomaPsych, supporting the expansion of trauma-informed and somatic education into community-centred trainings for vulnerable populations, minority communities, and professionals in demanding, client-facing roles. I am passionate and commitmed to collective care and to making embodied practice accessible beyond traditional wellness spaces.

events.long_description:
Directed by Rob Reiner
Starring Jerry O'Connell, Wil Wheaton, River Phoenix, Corey Feldman, Kiefer Sutherland
40th Anniversary
--- After learning that a stranger has been accidentally killed near their rural homes, four Oregon boys decide to go see the body. On the way, Gordie Lachance (Wil Wheaton), Vern Tessio (Jerry O'Connell), Chris Chambers (River Phoenix) and Teddy Duchamp (Corey Feldman) encounter a mean junk man and a marsh full of leeches, as they also learn more about one another and their very different home lives. Just a lark at first, the boys' adventure evolves into a defining event in their lives. ---
Upgrade your ticket to the FEAST YOUR EYES (FYE) experience which includes themed drink and dessert.
At Feast Your Eyes, cinephiles and foodies will be treated to a movie and food experience in the cinema at the perfect after dinner event!
Admit only tickets are $15, but you can upgrade to a Feast Your Eyes tickets for $45. All Feast Your Eyes tickets include a themed dessert* and cocktail to enjoy during the film.
The upstairs bar will open at 7:15pm for cocktail and dessert collection. The pre-show kicks off at 7:40pm and the feature begins at 7:50pm.
*No dietaries catered for.
Presented in 4K

events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
The Rogue's regular Sunday afternoon jazz. Funky, jazzy, soulful jazz. Beer, hangs, and jazz, on a Sunday, perfect!

This week featuring Ol' King Cole Quintet


Ol’ King Cole brings you the hits and celebrates the music of the great Nat ‘King’ Cole & performs the sounds of many other great tunes & artists of his era & more along the way. The group was originally inspired specifically with a focus on Nat’s early drumless trio which has now expanded for this special show and features Wellington based vocalist Wallace.

The core trio consisting of Robert Henderson on double bass along with Lockie Bennett on guitar and Louis Thompson-Munn on piano are now joined by prolific jazz drummer Mike Jensen.

A great and lively performance is always a guarantee with the spirit of the old house band alive, before TVs in bars and when people would dance the night away!

Music from 5pm, FREE ENTRY

events.id: Awa After Dark: Spotlighting in Central Park-Old Edwards Building, 131 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Awa After Dark: Spotlighting in Central Park
events.venue: Old Edwards Building, 131 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join experts from Mountain's to Sea Wellington's Wai Connection team, and the Te Papa Natural History team to explore your local park by night, at this free City Nature Challenge spotlighting event at Central Park, Brooklyn.
With your gumboots and a torch, head off on an evening hīkoi in your local park meet glowworm and ‘spotlight’ and observe freshwater species in their natural habitat. You'll also learn about the health of our waterways, freshwater conservation efforts in your neighbourhood, and some of the ways that you could become a kaitiaki of your local awa.
Awa After Dark events take place in the early evening, they’re perfect for the whole whānau.
This event is free, but bookings are essential. These events are very popular, please let us know if you have booked and can no longer make it so we can reallocate your spot.
Outdoor event: This event is outdoors, please dress appropriately (gumboots and warm clothing recommended). If the weather is really bad, we'll let you know we’re cancelling via the email you've provided us.
What is spotlighting? Ever played spotlight with a fish? Heaps of our native freshwater critters are nocturnal. Scientists often visit freshwater areas at nighttime with a torch, it’s the best time to see exactly who’s living in our awa!
What is Wai Connection? Wai Connection is a national programme delivered by Mountains to Sea Wellington aimed at connecting local communities to their waterways to improve them. We support communities and mana whenua with the knowledge, tools and expertise to monitor and care for their local freshwater places.

You'll also learn about the health of our waterways, freshwater conservation efforts in your neighbourhood, and some of the ways that you could become a kaitiaki of your local awa.

Stitch & Butch @ Blondini’s!
A casual meet up of crafters within the rainbow community
New members welcome.
Bring your knitting/crochet/cross stitch/embroidery/crafting/art or just come and have a chat.
Feel free to come partway through the time or leave early as needed.
Stitch and butch is a playful twist on the stitch and b!tch name to reflect the queerness of the group. Attendees do not need to be in any particular subset of the queer community to be welcome.
Usually gathered around one of the tables in the lounge area upstairs. Often near the walls for the brighter lights to better see our stitches.
There is a lift as well as stairs and Courtenay place bus stops across the road.
Held on the 2nd and 4th Mondays of the month with an end of year break.

events.id: Starve Your Distractions. Feed Your Focus.-175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Starve Your Distractions. Feed Your Focus.
events.venue: 175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Tuesday Wellbeing Seminars
Starve Your Distractions. Feed Your Focus.
In a world designed to pull our attention in every direction, how do we train the mind to focus on what actually matters? This workshop blends practical focus strategies with insights from the Bhagavad-gita on understanding and directing the mind.
Join Sivananda for a relaxed, discussion-based session exploring tools to manage attention and deeper wisdom for those interested in lasting clarity and fulfillment.
Meet your host Sivananda:
Sivananda’s background is in both science and music, holding a Master's Degree in Data Science, a Bachelor of Science in Physics and a long history of playing and performing on the double bass. His passion for philosophy, music, good food and community attracted him to the bhakti lifestyle. Having studied and practiced the science of the ‘self’ for six years now, he thrives on drawing insights from contemporary science and combining it with yoga wisdom to encourage others to explore new perspectives.
6.15pm Tuesday 28th April
End the session on a high note with a delicious plant-based dinner, dessert, and good conversation.
Tickets available here~ bit.ly/eventTickets
Bhakti Lounge 1/175 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington. http://www.bhaktilounge.org.nz
