events.id: Zentangle® Method - Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Zentangle® Method - Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Zentangle® Method - Beginners
The Zentangle® method is an easy-to-learn, relaxing and fun method to create amazing images by drawing structured patterns.
In this course you will learn to make beautiful art, following the 8 steps of the Zentangle® method from a certified Zentangle teacher.
Discover your creative side, even if you think you don’t have one. You might surprise yourself!  If you can hold a pen, you can do this.
Creating Zentangle patterns is super relaxing and a creative form of mindfulness. Increase your awareness and focus, get absorbed and feel more confident while making your own unique and original art.
Learn about borders, strings, and shading. Learn your first tangles and discover the world of Crescent Moon, Hollibaugh, Printemps, and Florz. It feels great to have creativity flowing. Anything is possible one stroke at a time. There are no mistakes to make here!
After a step-by-step introduction you will take home your amazing images and have discovered a new way to relax.
All art materials are included in course fee.  Tea and coffee is available but please bring your lunch/snacks.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-    follow the 8 steps of the Zentangle® method.
-    make beautiful structured black and white drawings.
-    explain why creating Zentangle patterns is a relaxing form of creative mindfulness.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: Cooking: Indian Homestyle
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Indian Homestyle
Delicious traditional Indian dishes (not based on ghee and cream!) are the focus in this four-session course where Manjula will share with you the recipes, flavours and aromas she grew up with.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: 
• Discuss the flavours and spices used in Indian and Gujarati cuisine
• Know where to purchase ingredients for a selection of vegetarian and meat dishes and finger food
• Read and follow a range of recipes step-by-step
• Create a range of classic dishes from Southern and Northern India as cooked at home
All ingredients included in course fee.
Bring an apron, dishcloth, and a couple of tea towels and four containers with lids to take any leftovers home.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.

events.id: Dita Von Teese-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Dita Von Teese
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
With her signature mystery and old-Hollywood glamour, Dita Von Teese is a global icon in the world of burlesque. She’s bringing an exciting new show to The Opera House in August. ‘Nocturnelle’ is a sensual spectacle inspired by the golden age of magic. It blends sophisticated striptease with the art of illusion for an unforgettable evening. Dita is accompanied by an ensemble of hand-picked neo-burlesque artists, so expect to be dazzled. 
Dita Von Teese is considered to have set the benchmark for American burlesque. The exotic and provocative art form has advanced under her unrivalled artistry and flair. The performer, creative director, and choreographer has sold out some of the world’s grandest venues, from Shanghai to New York. She is also a designer, collaborator, and entrepreneur. She’s forayed into fashion, beauty, music, and publishing.

events.id: Chris Parker — Take a Good Hard Look at Me-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Chris Parker — Take a Good Hard Look at Me
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
When comedian Chris Parker was young, he liked attention. He’s always endeavoured to make people look at him. In his latest show, he continues the theme of a lifetime. Take a Good Hard Look at Me displays the Kiwi comic’s sharp wit and clever timing. Chris says of his hour-long show — “There’s no secrets about to be dished, no valuable lesson to take away. I’m just a 35-year-old gay man, who was told there was something special about him as a kid and has been grappling with that ever since.” 
Chris is an actor, comedian, writer, and podcaster. He gained fame on TV’s Have You Been Paying Attention and Thank God You’re Here. He has written and performed extensively with Tom Sainsbury on live shows such as The Opening Night Before Christmas, Camping, Giggly Gerties, and Hauraki Horror. He also created and performed several sketch comedy shows with fellow comedian Hayley Sproull, including Tighty Whiteys, The Outsiders’ Guide, and Milky Bits.

events.id: Cooking: Lentils – Types, Recipes and How to Cook Them-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Lentils – Types, Recipes and How to Cook Them
The lentil is perhaps the world’s most versatile and indestructible food. It has significant health benefits from lowering cholesterol to supporting weight loss – and lentils are delicious when cooked well. Enjoy this half-day course that will show you how to cook lentils properly and make the most of this nutritious staple ingredient. Discover spices to complement and enhance lentils and create some mouth-watering dishes.
All ingredients included in course fee.  Please bring your apron, a couple of tea-towels, a sharp knife and leak-proof containers to take your food home in.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Recognise some different types of lentils.
Cook some delicious lentil dishes.
Understand some spices that will work well with lentils.
Follow simple recipes.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Suzanne Vega-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Suzanne Vega
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Widely regarded as one of the foremost songwriters of her generation, Suzanne Vega emerged as a leading figure of the folk revival of the early 1980s. Four decades later, she is still writing and singing great songs. In her Wellington show, Suzanne will perform a career-spanning set. See her on stage at St James Theatre performing favourites like Tom’s Diner, Luka, Marlene On The Wall, and plenty more. There’ll also be new songs from her 2025 album, ‘Flying with Angels’. Joining her on stage will be longtime guitarist, Gerry Leonard, and cellist Stephanie Winters. 
Since the release of her self-titled, critically acclaimed 1985 debut album, Suzanne Vega has played to sold-out concerts across the world. She is a masterful storyteller who, according to one The New York Times critique, “observes the world with a clinically poetic eye.” Her song lyrics often focus on city life, ordinary people, and real-world subjects. They are notably succinct, understated, and immediately recognisable.
events.description:
Widely regarded as one of the foremost songwriters of her generation, Suzanne Vega emerged as a leading figure of the folk revival of the early 1980s.
Four decades later, she is still writing and singing great songs.
In her Wellington show, Suzanne will perform a career-spanning set.
See her on stage at St James Theatre performing favourites like Tom’s Diner, Luka, Marlene On The Wall, and plenty more.
There’ll also be new songs from her 2025 album, ‘Flying with Angels’.

events.id: Verb Wellington Literary Festival--Wellington NZ
events.name: Verb Wellington Literary Festival
events.long_description:
Welcome in spring with a literary bonanza at Wellington’s iconic readers and writers’ festival. Verb has supported and nurtured Wellington’s arts community since its founding, and its annual festival has become a highlight of the literary calendar. Over three days, writers from across Aotearoa and several from around the world will share their ideas and stories with curious readers. Verb 2026 will be jam-packed with writers’ talks, workshops and panel discussions. 
Taking place from 11 to 13 September, Verb is a celebration of the written word and the community that surrounds it. See their website for a full list of the festival and special events.

events.id: Happy Mondays tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Happy Mondays tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Destroy All Lines
Pioneers of the iconic Madchester sound, the Happy Mondays are heading back to New Zealand to celebrate the 35th anniversary of their seminal album, Pills 'n' Thrills and Bellyaches.
Fusing funk, rock, psychedelia, and house with the pulsating energy of the UK’s burgeoning rave scene, the Happy Mondays became the face of a cultural revolution. Their 1990 release, Pills 'n' Thrills and Bellyaches, catapulted them into the mainstream, spending over six months on the UK Albums Chart and earning its place as one of the defining records of the decade.
Fronted by the inimitable Shaun Ryder, with Bez ( freaky dancing and percussion), Mark Day (guitar), Gary Whelan (drums), Mikey Shine (bass), Dan Broad (musical director/guitar/keys), and Firouzeh Berry (backing vocals), the band will perform highlights from the classic album; including anthems like Step On, Kinky Afro, and Loose Fit, alongside fan favourites such as 24 Hour Party People, Hallelujah, Judge Fudge, and W.F.L.
Their enduring influence on British music was formally recognised in 2016 when they received the prestigious Ivor Novello Inspiration Award, a testament to their legacy as one of the UK’s most beloved and groundbreaking bands.
‘Always a great buzz when we play Australia and New Zealand . Looking forward to bringing the Happy Mondays Pills ‘n’ Thrills party there in September’’ - Shaun Ryder
This isn’t just a tour, it’s a full-throttle revival of Madchester chaos, and the year's wildest party is coming.

events.id: World of WearableArt Show: GLO!-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: World of WearableArt Show: GLO!
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The World of WearableArt (WOW) Show is an internationally acclaimed annual event. Held every spring, WOW is New Zealand’s largest arena spectacle. This not-to-be-missed event features an electrifying combination of live music, cirque, dance and dazzling wearable art.
This year, the inspiration for WOW is GLO! It will see imagination run riot across six sections of pure, unfiltered, high-voltage entertainment. Expect live music, aerialists, dance, and of course, dazzling wearable art. Those six sections correspond with the competition categories at the heart of WOW. As the world’s premier wearable art competition, every garment on stage is a finalist. They are selected for imaginative storytelling and execution excellence. The competition offers a global platform for artists and designers and the chance to win over NZD$200,000 in prizes.

events.id: Cornelia Parker-City Gallery Wellington-Wellington NZ
events.name: Cornelia Parker
events.venue: City Gallery Wellington
events.long_description:
After three years of closure, the newly renovated and strengthened City Gallery Wellington Te Whare Toi will host the art of Cornelia Parker. The British visual artist is best known for her innovative and contemporary sculpture and installation art. Her large-scale, immersive installations explore themes of destruction and reconstruction, transformation and metamorphosis. Objects are exploded, crushed, and suspended in a world upended and remade anew.
Curated by independent UK-based curator Andrea Schlieker and City Gallery Wellington’s Senior Curator Aaron Lister, City Gallery Wellington’s exhibition will encompass Parker’s long career as an artist. This includes an immersive sound-and-light installation, ‘Stolen Thunder’. The work gathers the sounds of approaching storms from around the globe and across time. The exhibition will also showcase her paintings, photos, films, and sculptures.
“I have always said this is the one exhibition I want to do before I die,” said Charlotte Davy, Ringatohu Toi Director, Art and City Gallery Wellington. “I have been following Cornelia Parker’s work for the last twenty years. Bringing her mischievous, dark, and literally explosive work to Wellington is incredible.” 
Parker’s work often fills entire rooms and invites the viewer to move through and around the art. Given its physical scale, the blank canvas of the reopened gallery offers the perfect opportunity to host the exhibition. The exhibition will kick off in October 2026 and run through March 2027. 
Charlotte notes the exhibition’s themes are also of particular relevance to Wellington. “I hope that the exhibition and our return to Te Ngākau Civic Square will reflect our city and also help remake it. We are looking forward to opening with a bang.”
Hero image: © Cornelia Parker. Courtesy of the artist and Frith Street Gallery, London

events.id: Songwriting: How to Write Your Own Songs-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Songwriting: How to Write Your Own Songs
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Songwriting: How to Write Your Own Songs
The course is ideal for anyone who wants to write better songs - whether you’ve got a few songs under your belt or you’re a complete beginner. Take a song idea through to a finished song you can be proud of. Split into easy-to-follow steps, this course walks you through everything from finding titles to writing chord progressions and ensuring your lyrics and melodies fit together. You’ll share your creations with fellow songwriters and get personalised feedback. You’ll see exactly how to turn theory into practice and write your best songs yet.
This course includes:
-  Practical tips and techniques for songwriting
-  Ideas for making your songs stronger, more expressive and more appealing to your audience
-  How to overcome writer’s block, be an inspired and prolific songwriter who writes authentic songs
-  An opportunity to share your songs with fellow students, bounce off ideas and learn from one another
-  Learn how to structure a song effectively
-  The pillars of songwriting and how to use them
-  How to write better lyrics
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-  Discover the exact steps to successfully write your own original songs; expanding a song from a verse and chorus, to a complete song while keeping your listeners engaged using transitions and dynamic contrasts between sections
-  Come up with ideas, write lyrics, melodies, chord progressions and instrumental grooves that have an emotional impact on you and your audience
-  Identify the structure of a great song, including how to combine melody, chords, lyrics and rhythm that create magic for your listeners
-  Possess the confidence and skills to find inspiration and get past common creative hurdles and blocks so you can develop an initial spark of creativity into a complete song
-  Stay inspired and find your authentic voice as a songwriter. r
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: Latin American Dance
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Latin American Dance
Join Alex White from "Viva Latina" dance school and learn some Spanish language and culture through dance and, to keep it as authentic as possible, many of the dance sequence names will be in Spanish as well as English. We’ll be focusing on Salsa and Tango, but will also look at the basics of other common Latin dance styles: Merengue, Bachata, Kizomba and Zouk.
Couples are welcome but partners are not essential (please note however that we cannot guarantee equal numbers of men and women).
By the end of the course, you'll be able to:  
• Demonstrate basic steps
• Confidently lead or follow basic turn patterns and key fundamentals in Salsa and Tango
• Identify and practise the basic movement characteristics of Zouk, Bachata, Kizomba and Merengue  
• Recognise different styles and tempos of Latin dance music
Wear comfortable clothing and shoes suitable for dancing and a bottle of water.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. events.name: Scriptwriting
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Scriptwriting
During this course, you will develop and build on your writing skills so you can confidently produce scripted narrative. You will learn how the basics of scriptwriting work, whether it be for stage or screen.
 Topics discussed and practised include the three act structure, script format, the differences between writing for film, theatre and television, creating dialogue, world building and more.
 We’ll take part in exercises, watch clips and have group discussions. In workshops, you’ll give and receive feedback, developing your scriptwriting skills through both. By the end of the course, you’ll be ready to finish your script, and take the next steps toward bringing it from page to stage (or screen!).
 Bring pen and paper.
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Analyse film, television, and theatre with a new lens for the way scripted stories work
- Develop character arcs and dynamics
- Create and build worlds
- Write effective dialogue and work with subtext
- Develop and implement a writing routine that works for your life
- Give and receive constructive feedback on your writing
- Have the confidence to put your work out into the world
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Genealogy - Basics of ancestry.com DNA tests-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Genealogy - Basics of ancestry.com DNA tests
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Genealogy - Basics of ancestry.com DNA tests
Got your test results back and still wondering what a centiMorgan or a shared match is?  Find out what the size of your match means and the tools available to help you work out possible family relationships.
We'll start by looking at Clustering and how the Grouping tool on Ancestry.com can help with this.  We'll also look at the ThruLines and Common Ancestors tools to see how they can provide suggestions for knocking down brick walls in your research.
Also covered during this course are other websites you can upload your results to, how to manage other family member's DNA test results and other tools available when you get a bit more advanced - and just in case you need help in the future, we'll look at some of the places you could get this from.
Please note:  this course will only cover DNA tests taken through Ancestry.com  All computer courses are run in the school computer labs and you are required to use the school desktops for the duration of the course (you are not able to use your own laptop).  Some of the courses may require you to login onto the network. 
Bring a USB stick and your Ancestry.com login details.
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-  Understand the information provided by Ancestry.com about your DNA matches
-  Use the Grouping, ThruLines and Common Ancestors tools on Ancestry.com
-  Use the Shared CentiMorgan tool on DNAPainter.com
-  Understand where to find other tools
-  Understand the options for using other DNA websites
-  Understand where to get further help
 Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Bjorn Again: ABBA Forever Tour-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Bjorn Again: ABBA Forever Tour
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The only ABBA-endorsed tribute show hits Wellington for one spectacular night. Björn Again has been delighting audiences worldwide for over 35 years. They’ve played more than 7,000 shows in over 130 countries to great acclaim. Regarded as the most successful tribute act of all time, members of ABBA have endorsed Björn Again’s outstanding production. You can expect hits such as SOS, Fernando, Take a Chance on Me, Knowing Me Knowing You, Super Trouper, Waterloo, Money Money Money, Dancing Queen, and many more.
Rolling Stone magazine describes them as “fabulously flawless”. Say Gimmie Gimmie Gimmie to the chance to go to a night of 70s Swedish pop nostalgia.

events.id: Greg Davies — Full Fat Legend-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Greg Davies — Full Fat Legend
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
He’s six feet and eight inches tall and has a towering sense of humour. After a seven-year break, comedian Greg Davies is back on tour, and he’s heading to Wellington. His fresh new show, Full Fat Legend, promises a wild walk through the beloved UK comic’s mind. Expect a run-through of the more embarrassing situations he finds himself in, plus memories from his childhood in the 1970s. This will all be delivered with his characteristic wit, along with a healthy dose of toilet humour.   
Greg hasn’t been on the comedy circuit for a while — he’s been busy. You are likely to have seen him hosting Taskmaster, presenting Never Mind The Buzzcocks, or starring in his sitcom The Cleaner.

events.id: Creedence Clearwater Collective-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Creedence Clearwater Collective
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Take a walk back in time to enjoy some of the most famous music of the 60s and 70s. Creedence Clearwater Revival was a juggernaut of phenomenal proportions. From their album debut in 1968 to their last album in 1972 the band produced some highly memorable songs. Many of them are still regularly played today. The Creedence Clearwater Collective recreates the success of the original band. The St James Theatre stage in Wellington will come alive with a six-piece tribute band. There’ll be a guest saxophonist and a vocalist who emulates the distinct sounds of original vocalist John Fogerty. 
The night promises to be full of iconic tunes including songs they wrote and songs they covered. Listen to hits like Have You Ever Seen The Rain, Fortunate Son, Bad Moon Rising, and Proud Mary.

events.id: Fly Fishing-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Fly Fishing
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Fly Fishing
An introduction to fly fishing for trout, which includes choosing gear, finding the fish, fly tying and casting and a practical fishing session.
By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
• Apply the basics of fly fishing
• Choose the appropriate tackle
• Have an appreciation of the entomology associated with trout
• Demonstrate practical aspects of casting, knot tying, tying of flies and stream craft
• Present an appropriate fly to the water with a reasonable chance of catching a trout
There are three sessions at the high school, plus a session at the Capital Trout Centre and a choice of two river sessions (times and dates to be discussed at the class). Tutors from Wellington Flyfishers Club.   There may also be additional opportunity before class (from 6pm) for casting practice before sessions 2 and 3.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Get Rhythm — The Johnny Cash & June Carter Show-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Get Rhythm — The Johnny Cash & June Carter Show
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s more than just a tribute concert — it’s a live theatrical experience bringing to life the heart, humour, and deep connection of a famous country music couple. Get Rhythm — The Johnny Cash & June Carter Show is coming to Wellington for the very first time in 2026. 
 
Based on the award-winning movie ‘Walk The Line’, Get Rhythm stars real-life married duo Mark and Joanne Caligiuri. Together, they capture the look, sound, and spirit of Johnny and June with remarkable authenticity. Between songs, heartfelt storytelling and charming onstage chemistry, you will be taken on a beautiful musical journey. Authentic, emotional, and unmissable — Get Rhythm stands as a powerful and respectful celebration of Johnny Cash and June Carter’s legacy.

events.id: Posture Perfection is Possible!-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Posture Perfection is Possible!
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Posture Perfection is Possible!
Are you a computer slouch, feeling stiff and tense at work? Do you find it difficult to maintain a good posture?  Does your posture cause back, neck or shoulder tension?  Would you like to enjoy more freedom in everyday activities?
Under the guidance of an experienced teacher, we'll explore movement in ways that help you reduce tense unhelpful habits.  Movement lessons are done on the floor or sitting on a chair and are based on the pioneering work of Israeli physicist and engineer Dr. Moshe Feldenkrais.
This workshop is suitable for all ages (over 16) and fitness levels.
By the end of the workshop, you'll be able to:
• Identify and reduce muscle tension
• Move with more lightness and freedom
• Improve your posture
• Describe some basic anatomy
• Have tools to use at home and at work
Bring two blankets (or a mat and a blanket) and wear loose, warm and comfortable clothing. Tea and coffee provided but bring your lunch. Please note that instructions are given verbally rather than by demonstration, so good hearing and understanding of English is required.
Feel free to contact the tutor, Sue Field on 027 466 7123, if you have any questions. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Crochet - Christmas Special!-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Crochet - Christmas Special!
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Crochet - Christmas Special!
Join Monique in this half-day crochet workshop where you will learn how to crochet a range of little projects suitable for the holiday season.  Choose from stars, icicles, mini wreaths and Christmas jumpers, Christmas trees and a mini Santa hat!   There will be a booklet of patterns for you to take home, to enable you to complete in your own time the projects that you didn’t have time to make during the workshop.
  You will also learn how to crochet and decorate Christmas baubles!  While you might not finish all the patterns this day, the aim is to have a go at making as many as possible!
  You will need to know some basic crochet stitches such as single and double crochet. You’ll also need to have some understanding of how to read a pattern and work with diagrams, as taught in the beginners’ crochet course.
  Please bring yarn that you enjoy working with (cotton, acrylic or wool, 4ply or 8 ply) in a variety of colours (red, white and green, a neutral colour for the mouse, and glittery yarns are all good options for the Christmas projects) and a matching crochet hook. You’ll also need a pair of scissors and a darning needle.
  Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Harakeke (Flax Weaving) Workshop
Gain an introduction to the Māori art of flax weaving in an intensive one-day workshop, suitable for beginners and those with some experience.   
By the end of the workshop, you'll be able to:
•  Identify commonly-used types of harakeke
•  Describe how to harvest and prepare harakeke for use 
•  Describe the tikanga (the protocol) surrounding the use of harakeke and weaving 
•  Demonstrate some basic weaving techniques  
•  Construct and complete a small project 
Materials provided but please bring a 'bread and butter' knife and 8 spring-loaded clothes pegs. Tea and coffee provided but please bring your lunch.
[A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend)
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cheesemaking - Farmhouse Cheddar Cheese Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cheesemaking - Farmhouse Cheddar Cheese Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cheesemaking - Farmhouse Cheddar Cheese Workshop
During this hands-on workshop, you’ll be guided through each step of the cheesemaking process while making your very own round of farmhouse style cheddar.
The fee includes a cheese press (value $95.00) which can be used every time you want to make a hard cheese. It holds up to 6 litres of milk and this is what you’ll be taking your cheese home in to press for 12 hours after the workshop. All ingredients, recipes and a full set of course notes are provided.
You’ll also receive comprehensive instruction on how to wax (if you choose to) and then mature your cheese over the next five weeks. Alexis will also demonstrate how to make halloumi cheese which will then be grilled for you to enjoy! There’ll be a short lunch break and cheese making supplies such as wax will be available to purchase.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
• Describe the basics of sanitisation and hygiene
• Recognise the difference between raw and pasteurised milk and know where to source them
• Make a round of cheddar cheese using a cheese press
• Describe how to wax and mature the cheese
• Describe how to make halloumi, following a demonstration 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Jesus Christ Superstar-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Jesus Christ Superstar
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s a 70s rock musical of biblical proportions, and it’s back. Jesus Christ Superstar is heading to St James Theatre, and Wellington audiences are in for a treat. This production won the 2017 Olivier Award for Best Musical Revival and came fresh off an acclaimed 2023/24 tour of the United Kingdom. 
Jesus Christ Superstar explores the biblical events leading to the death and resurrection of Jesus Christ as seen through the eyes of his betrayer, Judas Iscariot. The story, told entirely through song, explores the personal relationships and struggles among Jesus, Judas, Mary Magdalene, Jesus’ disciples, his followers and the Roman Empire.
The 1970s rock score features lyrics and music by Emmy, GRAMMY, Oscar, and Tony winners Tim Rice and Andrew Lloyd Webber. Well-known numbers include I Don’t Know How to Love Him, Gethsemane, and Superstar.

events.id: An Introduction to Self-Hypnosis - Unlock Your Inner Power-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: An Introduction to Self-Hypnosis - Unlock Your Inner Power
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Self-Hypnosis - Hypnotise Yourself
Self-hypnosis is a fantastic self-development tool. It can also be used for controlling pain, accelerated healing, letting go of limiting behaviours and a number of other applications. This workshop will provide you with a good insight into the world of hypnosis, demystify it and dispel the myths that surround it.
You’ll learn a structured method of taking yourself into hypnosis (informed by scientific research), some of the classic applications of self-hypnosis and evidence-based skills that will convince you without doubt of your ability to hypnotise yourself.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
• Define and discuss hypnosis
• Practice self-hypnosis
• Create and write hypnosis scripts for personal change
• Use the language of self-hypnosis
• Relax hypnotically to deep levels
Tea and coffee provided but bring your lunch, pen and paper.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Beekeeping - Next Step-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Beekeeping - Next Step
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Beekeeping - Next Step
This is a practical and hands-on experience so bring your beekeeper’s outfit and your lunch. Please check your course instructions for more information on what you need to bring on the day.  The tutor can lend you a veil if you don't have one, please let the CEC office know a week before the course is due to run so this can be arranged.
The workshop starts in the classroom and is followed by a visit to a local apiary.  (Please note that you will working with live bees during the visit so there is a chance of being stung by one.)
If weather isn't suitable on the day, the morning session will still go ahead but the visit will be postponed to the next available Saturday.   Please note that if you can't make the catch-up visit, it's not possible to join in later courses due to number restrictions (a part-refund will be made). You must have done the Beginners' course first.
By the end of the workshop, you'll be able to:
• List the key steps in setting up a hive
• Describe how honey is extracted
• State what causes swarming and some ways of preventing it
• Describe what needs to be done to manage Varoa and American Foul Brood 
• Construct kitset frames for a honey super
• Open up and inspect a hive 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: Richard Dawkins — 50 Years of The Selfish Gene
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Richard Dawkins is a world-renowned evolutionary biologist. When published in 1976, his book, ‘The Selfish Gene’, reframed our understanding of life, behaviour, and human nature. Now, 50 years later, he is coming to Wellington to reflect on the book’s revolutionary legacy. He explores what fifty years of science has confirmed or challenged and explains why its core questions are more urgent today than ever before. 
‘The Selfish Gene’ has remained a cornerstone of modern science. It introduced the gene-centred view of evolution and coined the now-ubiquitous concept of the ‘meme’. The ideas in it ignited global debate and five decades of subsequent research. Richard Dawkins is one of the most consequential scientific communicators of our time, so don’t miss the rare opportunity to hear him speak in person.

events.id: Cooking: Singapore Nyonya Food-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Singapore Nyonya Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Singapore Nyonya Food
Create some classic mouth-watering and flavourful Nyonya dishes. Come and discover how to blend ingredients with distinct spices and cooking techniques. You can easily replicate these dishes at home. Singaporean cooking is characterised by being mildly spiced, but you can always adjust to your preference.
Some ingredients supplied but a list of additional ingredients needed (approximate cost $35) will be sent out when course numbers are confirmed.
Please bring an apron, dish cloth, sharp knife, tea towels and containers to take food home.
Target audience: Adventurous food lovers!
NOTE: Nuts and gluten will be used in some recipes - this course is not suitable for vegetarians.
By the end of the course, you’ll have:
• Made a selection of Nyonya dishes to take home.
• Become more familiar with using the various spices.
• Understood more about the Nyonya cooking.
• Become more confident to cook Nyonya food at home.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Urzila Carlson — Fatty On a Yacht-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Urzila Carlson — Fatty On a Yacht
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
She’s known for her deadpan delivery and understated punchlines. Fan-favourite comedian Urzila Carlson is back in time for Christmas with a brand-new show — Fatty On a Yacht. Ever felt like you don’t belong? Like everyone else got the memo — and you turned up in the wrong outfit, on the wrong day, on the wrong yacht? According to Urzila, Fatty On a Yacht is for anyone who’s ever tried to squeeze into a space — physical or social — and thought, “This can’t be right”. 
The multi-award-winning South African-New Zealander is one of Australasia’s biggest comedy stars. In 2025, she won the prestigious People’s Choice Award for most tickets sold at the Melbourne International Comedy Festival for an incredible fourth time, after wins in 2019, 2022 and 2023. In 2019, she was presented with the Rielly Comedy Award from the Variety Artists Club of New Zealand for her contribution to New Zealand entertainment.

events.id: Paul Smith: Happy-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Paul Smith: Happy
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s billed as the biggest and boldest show of his career. Paul Smith’s show Happy is set to visit Wellington’s Michael Fowler Centre in February 2027. Happy takes Paul’s trademark crowd work, razor-sharp observations, and laugh-out-loud true stories to an entirely new level. This is stand-up at full throttle: fast, fearless, and relentlessly funny. 
The show is packed with outrageous audience interaction and stories pulled straight from real life. It’s about finding joy in the chaos, laughter in the everyday, and comedy in absolutely everything. 
Liverpool-born Paul Smith is known for his quick wit and unrivalled ability to roast audience members. With over 5,000 videos online, he holds the unofficial record for the most online stand-up content and has amassed a massive global following. Happy will be his sixth stand-up tour.

events.id: The Human League — Generations: The Greatest Hits Tour-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: The Human League — Generations: The Greatest Hits Tour
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A fresh and influential sound dominated the charts in the 1980s — synth-pop. The Human League were one of the first bands to find success with this new sound. Now they are bringing decades of catchy hits to Wellington fans. Expect an unforgettable night with timeless classics like Don’t You Want Me, Human, Love Action (I Believe in Love), The Lebanon, Mirror Man, Being Boiled, and more. 
Long before electronic music became mainstream, the band was successfully using computers and sequencers to create rhythm and melody. In the process, they redefined modern pop. Their stylish, forward-thinking sound remains relevant today as ever. In 1982, The Sydney Morning Herald described them as “one of the first futurist bands.” Join founding members Philip Oakey, Joanne Catherall, and Susan Ann Sulley on an electrifying journey through four decades of stylish synth-pop brilliance.

events.id: CubaDupa--Wellington NZ
events.name: CubaDupa
events.long_description:
One weekend every March, CubaDupa takes over some of Wellington’s central city streets for an epic party. The free annual festival brings a carnival vibe to the end of the summer. It offers live music, street theatre, dance, parades, and other cultural experiences. 
CubaDupa is New Zealand’s largest outdoor arts and music festival. It takes place over two days on Cuba Street and the surrounding roads, right in the heart of Wellington. The festival is suitable for the whole family with performances and activities running throughout the day and night. Multiple performance spaces showcase New Zealand’s emerging musical talent alongside established artists. The lineup usually includes over 140 local and international acts.

events.id: Kia Mau--Wellington NZ
events.name: Kia Mau
events.long_description:
Kia Mau is an arts festival that showcases innovative Indigenous performances. Held every two years, the festival lights up Wellington's winter. See contemporary performances by leading Tāngata Whenua , Tāngata Moana, and global Indigenous artists. The two-week festival includes dance, theatre, music, and visual art. Audiences can expect to be challenged and thrilled by new work from Aotearoa and beyond.
Kia Mau was first held in 2015, founded by two leading Aotearoa creatives. Hone Kouka (Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Kahungunu, Ngāti Raukawa, Kai Tahu) is the Artistic Director and Chief Executive. Mīria George (Ngāti Kearoa, Ngāti Tuara, Ngāti Awa, Ngāti Kūki ‘Āirani) is the Executive Director.
Hone and Mīria describe themselves as innovators. They push boundaries, redefine forms, and forge new paths. They say the festival is not just a platform for presentations, it’s a hub for kōrero and whanaungatanga . The festival has a strong influence on many artists and plays an important role in shaping New Zealand’s contemporary arts scene. “Kia Mau has continued to thrive because of the imagination and commitment of our artists. And of us to them,” says Mīria.

events.id: Black Ferns vs British and Irish Lions-Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Black Ferns vs British and Irish Lions
events.venue: Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The first-ever Howden British and Irish Lions women‘s series is coming to Aotearoa in 2027. With five games across the country, Wellington is to play host to one of three Test games. The competition is sure to be fierce and the atmosphere electric. 
Sitting at the halfway point between Rugby World Cups, the tour will offer high-calibre clashes between two rugby powerhouses. Ben Calveley, CEO of The British & Irish Lions, said the tour marks “something truly special for the women’s game.” With the first-ever tour title on the table, you can expect both teams to leave it all on the field. Let‘s fill the cake tin with stomping feet and edge-of-your-seat screams. The Wellington game is Test number two of the series, so pull on your jersey, warm up your pipes and get ready for Black Ferns fever to sweep the city. 
Tickets go on sale late 2026.
